races.race_name: CAA Stellar Lily Agnes EBF Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hickory Lad
runners.spotlight: Made all to beat two rivals at Musselburgh (5f, good) last week, going one better than he did at Thirsk; faces competition for the lead to his inside but is otherwise one of the main form contenders.
runners.comment: Made all at Musselburgh last week, building on his Thirsk effort

runners.name: Final Appeal
runners.spotlight: Already proven on a turning, left-handed track having recorded a comfortable win from the front at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) to build on his Nottingham effort; looks a very suitable type.
runners.comment: Gained his AW win at left-handed, turning Wolverhampton; looks a very suitable type

runners.name: Wait Geordie
runners.spotlight: Notable contender, being a once-raced novice winner who represents a local yard that targets Chester; made all at Bath (5f, good) and should be handy from his low draw; commands respect.
runners.comment: Once-raced novice winner (at Bath) who represents a local yard that targets Chester

runners.name: Adonius
runners.spotlight: Two from two, namely wins at Musselburgh (5f, soft/good to soft) by 7l then a neck; made all and always doing enough under a penalty last time; big player from the inside gate.
runners.comment: Two from two, namely wins at Musselburgh; big player from the inside gate

runners.name: Black Treasure
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros yearling; by Blackbeard; related to a few 2yo scorers (7f/1m) but inexperience may count against her, being the sole newcomer in the field; check the betting for further guidance.
runners.comment: 30,000euros yearling; sole newcomer in the field; heed the market signals

runners.name: Cailin Aine
runners.spotlight: Showed ability with third-place finish at Redcar (5f, good to firm) but recorded only an ordinary RPR; drawn wider than the main form contenders and faces a difficult task.
runners.comment: Recorded only an ordinary RPR in finishing third at Redcar

runners.name: Fern Clyde
runners.spotlight: Seemed to need the outing/experience at Beverley (5f, good to soft) where she finished sixth; however, needs a big step forward in terms of form and has the worst of the draw.
runners.comment: Needs to step up plenty on her Beverley effort

runners.name: Yahaira
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form, having posted a duck egg at Bath (5f, good); perhaps needed the run but this looks a stiff assignment.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form, having posted a duck egg at Bath

races.race_name: Join Ladbrokes Bet 5 Get 30 Earl Grosvenor Handicap

runners.name: Percy's Lad
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer with a very good Chester record, including a win in this race on good to firm in 2024 (also third on soft in 2023); off for 20 months prior to finishing well held at the Newmarket Craven meeting three weeks ago but he led for long enough to think plenty of ability remains and this has presumably been the target; could bounce back to form with a bang.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024; led for long way on comeback run; could be primed for another bold bid

runners.name: Lir Speciale
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign with a heavy-ground 7f double at Doncaster; underlined how well he operates on Town Moor with a good third of 22 in the Spring Mile (good to soft) on reappearance in March; claims if he can reproduce that sort of form at Chester (first visit).
runners.comment: Two 7f Doncaster wins in mud in late 2025; also good third of 22 there on 1m reappearance

runners.name: Divine Libra
runners.spotlight: Has gained both handicap wins at Chester, including over the bare 7f at this meeting (good) in May 2024; respectable fifth in this last year; a reappearance run over 6f at the Newmarket Craven meeting could have put him spot on for this; likely to be in the mix here again.
runners.comment: Good Chester record includes 7f win at this meeting in 2024; should be sharper for return

runners.name: Snow Master
runners.spotlight: Impressed when winning two fast-ground novices at Yarmouth (7f/1m) just over a year apart; too keen when sixth to Zeus Olympios in 1m Group races at Haydock (led) and Newmarket (held up) in the autumn; has switched from Tom Dascombe to Andrew Balding ahead of reappearance (same owners); also gelded which could help to settle him down; Double Rush's exploits this spring underline the yard's prowess with new recruits and this one has similar potential.
runners.comment: Promising for Tom Dascombe and interesting new recruit for top yard having been gelded

runners.name: God Of War
runners.spotlight: Never involved back on turf at Newmarket last month; however, he returned with two respectable efforts on AW prior to that and the fact he's dipped to 1lb below the mark he defied at Sandown (7f, soft) last summer and was runner-up at this meeting last year make him a dangerous one to discount.
runners.comment: Below par latest but on winning mark and second at this meeting last year; can't discount

runners.name: Partisan Hero
runners.spotlight: All wins have been over 7f and 1m so it arguably bodes well for his prospects here that he's performed well over sprint trips in Bahrain this year; very good second in this race (good ground) last year from an outside stall and this front-runner has fared a lot better with the draw this time; top of the list.
runners.comment: Second in this from wide stall in 2025 and this front-runner is much better drawn now

runners.name: Bellarchi
runners.spotlight: Last four wins at Musselburgh, including two last month, latterly producing a career-best performance to see off ten rivals over 1m on good; runner-up on only Chester visit; a further 4lb rise demands even more but can't rule out in her current mood.
runners.comment: In career-best form with two Musselburgh wins last month; can't rule out in current mood

runners.name: Thunder Roar
runners.spotlight: Very useful handicapper who added to his tally with a 1m win at Ayr (soft) last September; ended that campaign with an excellent second at York but not at his best in two comeback outings in March; third on his previous visit to Chester but will do well to match or better that from the outside stall.
runners.comment: Very useful but below par both runs this year and has the outside stall

runners.name: The Angel King
runners.spotlight: Useful dual 7f winner (both on ground slower than good) in France but well held on recent 7f Haydock stable debut; needs to have come on a lot in a short time.
runners.comment: Useful dual 7f winner in France but no obvious promise on recent yard debut

runners.name: Brighton Boy
runners.spotlight: Had a below-par Al Shabab Storm in behind when winning a useful C&D seller (soft) for Andrew Balding last September; made a respectable start for new connections when seventh of 12 over 7f at Thirsk (good) 18 days ago; entitled to come on for the outing but the draw hasn't done him any favours here.
runners.comment: C&D winner; should come on for recent yard debut but stall 12 is far from ideal.

runners.name: Supido
runners.spotlight: Fifth over C&D (good) at this meeting last year on his first outing for Ian Williams and followed that with a very good fifth of 30 in the Britannia at Royal Ascot (1m, good to firm); failed to match that level later in the campaign but his mark has dipped and dangerous if bouncing back to form after a winter break; a good draw and Billy Loughnane in the saddle are positives.
runners.comment: Well treated on form early last season; has good draw and B Loughnane riding; contender

runners.name: City House
runners.spotlight: Smart turf form at up to in Bahrain for George Scott early last year; best run for Adrian McGuinness when second over 6f at Dundalk in February and bounced back from a lesser effort at Newcastle on Good Friday when fifth of 12 on return to turf at Naas (6f, good) last Monday; has a handy draw but not obviously well treated on recent exploits.
runners.comment: Creditable fifth on turf latest but more needed here; does have a handy draw, though

runners.name: Solar Aclaim
runners.spotlight: Useful sprinter, landing soft-ground wins over 6f here and 5f at Yarmouth last summer; has had wind surgery ahead of reappearance; a new trip and a wide stall and are enough reasons to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: 6f course winner but new trip and wide stall enough reasons to oppose; has had wind op

runners.name: Al Shabab Storm
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Listed race here (good to soft) for Andrew Balding in 2024; still capable of smart performances for Marco Botti last year but wasn't at his best on final two starts, including in a course seller; sold for 45,000gns in October; a Group 1 entry at Royal Ascot suggests new connections are hopeful he retains all his ability.
runners.comment: Course Listed winner at peak but ended 2025 out of form; new yard

races.race_name: Weatherbys Cheshire Oaks (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Amelia Earhart
runners.spotlight: Started favourite in all five juvenile appearances, culminating in an easy success in Leopardstown maiden (1m, yielding) that was won 12 months earlier by the same yard's Minnie Hauk, who followed up in this race en route to even bigger things; sister to a Chester Cup winner for her trainer, whose Cheshire Oaks record is impressive; brings strong credentials and has to be feared, assuming she takes to first-time hood/blinkers.
runners.comment: Ballydoyle filly; has strong credentials based on her Leopardstown win and her pedigree
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Sugar Island
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly whose 2yo campaign was bookended by Curragh wins, latest comfortably in Group 3 contest over 1m on heavy ground; the form pick even with a 5lb penalty, albeit some of today's rivals (including stablemate Amelia Earhart) could reach or surpass her level.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner; the form pick but some of today's rivals could reach or surpass her level
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: I'm The One
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred filly, by Sea The Stars and the first foal of a German 1m4f Group 1 winner; impressively justified favouritism in Newbury maiden (1m2f, good) last month for a highly promising debut, striding clear in the final 1f to score by 6l; very bright prospect who is currently ante-post favourite for the Oaks and commands major respect.
runners.comment: Impressive debut performance in Newbury maiden; favourite for the Oaks; highly respected

runners.name: Winged One
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward to win well at Newcastle (1m, AW) in the second of her two autumn starts; however, she's the only runner who isn't entered in the Oaks and, as her bare form backs up, perhaps brings the least ability and/or potential.
runners.comment: Big step forward to win on AW but perhaps brings the least ability and/or potential

runners.name: A La Prochaine
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega filly who cost 550,000gns as a yearling; appeared to win with something in hand at Newbury (1m, soft) in sole 2yo start; promising sort who could make her mark at this higher level, albeit rather lacks the dazzle of the other once-raced contender, I'm The One.
runners.comment: Appeared to win with something in hand at Newbury in sole 2yo start; promising sort

races.race_name: Boodles Chester Vase Stakes (Group 3) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Benvenuto Cellini
runners.spotlight: Made all in a maiden last July and most impressive in four-runner Group 2 at Leopardstown (1m, good to yielding) in September; made favourite for the Derby on the back of that, and still has that position despite his third of five behind two stablemates when warm favourite for Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m) in October, which was on heavy ground; sister Giselle won last year's Lingfield Oaks Trial (Oaks fifth); their trainer is the first one to turn to for winners of these Chester Derby and Oaks trials; hard to beat.
runners.comment: Most impressive in 1m Group 2 at Leopardstown in September; Group 1 Futurity 3rd on heavy

runners.name: Del Maro
runners.spotlight: Derby entry; progressive as 2yo (hooded three times) while always finding one or two too good for him, in the last instance when third behind two Aidan O'Brien-trained colts in Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm); it's unlikely he had to reproduce that form when seeing off a newcomer by half a length in novice at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) on reappearance, at 4-7; not the most exciting on that evidence but likely to relish this extra distance.
runners.comment: Off the mark on reappearance; best form when third in Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f) as 2yo

runners.name: Mr Colonel
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck on first two runs before his front-running and rallying fifth of eight in Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm; sole run in cheekpieces) in October, just over 2l behind third-placed Del Maro; clear before halfway when 11-10 for a four-runner novice at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) 16 days ago but had to battle for a short-head win; while he still looks a work in progress and 1m4f+ is likely to suit, he'd be a surprise winner of this.
runners.comment: Just over 2l behind Del Maro on final 2yo start; short-head win on return; 1m4f+ can suit

runners.name: Mr Vettori
runners.spotlight: Irish Derby entry whose dam is sister to Yorkshire Oaks and Vermeille winner Warm Heart; hung markedly right on his last two starts as a 2yo when 50-1 second at the Curragh (good) to subsequent Group 1 Futurity winner Hawk Mountain and going one better in another 1m maiden at Gowran (heavy);  made a satisfactory return when second of four in a conditions event at Cork (10.5f, good) 12 days ago; can build on that but he needs to does so in a major way.
runners.comment: Useful middle-distance prospect, at the least, but this race demands a marked step forward

runners.name: Proposition
runners.spotlight: Has big-race entries; Frankel close relation to multiple Group 1-winning fillies Magical (up to 1m4f) and Rhododendron (up to 1m2f); well held on debut but second favourite for a maiden at Galway (7f, soft to heavy) later in October and eventually got the better of an odds-on stablemate who's not been seen since; cheekpieces go on and he's presumably the stable second string, but surely set for big improvement over middle distances this term.
runners.comment: Galway 2yo maiden winner (7f, soft to heavy); surely set for big improvement; cheekpieces
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

races.race_name: American Holidays Handicap

runners.name: Alaminos
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt whose form features a debut success last August and neck second, looking unlucky, at Pontefract (6f, good; had traffic issues) on handicap/seasonal debut; remains open to further improvement and commands respect.
runners.comment: Unlucky second at Pontefract on handicap/seasonal debut; still open to further improvement
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Watcha Snoop
runners.spotlight: Made all at Ascot (6f, soft) to build on debut effort; subsequently down the field in the Mill Reef and a valuable race on Champions Day but may be capable of progress back down in class; local runner who is interesting on handicap debut.
runners.comment: May be capable of progress back down in class; interesting on handicap debut

runners.name: Tricky Tel
runners.spotlight: Career commenced with two wins at Chester (5.5f/6f, good) last May; not in the same form elsewhere subsequently but the return to this venue, combined with a gelding operation, may prompt a resurgence; one of three runners for his local stable.
runners.comment: Return to Chester may prompt a resurgence; 2-2 here, both starts last May

runners.name: Ruby's Angel
runners.spotlight: Absent since a below-par effort switched to AW (7f) eight months ago; opened her account in competitive nursery at the York Ebor festival (6f, good to firm; led final strides) the time before and may be capable of better still on turf; this local runner is shortlisted.
runners.comment: Won competitive nursery at the York Ebor festival in last turf attempt; likely player

runners.name: Angel Love
runners.spotlight: Comfortably justified favouritism at left-handed, turning Wolverhampton (kept to 6f on AW) in final 2yo start, stepping up on her placed efforts; interesting contender on turf/handicap debut, being one who could take well to Chester and improve further.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in final 2yo start; could take well to Chester and improve further

runners.name: Temple Of Athena
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 5f, winning three times, including at Chester (good to soft); not disgraced in Listed event at Bath most recently; well drawn but the return to 6f presents a question mark.
runners.comment: Three-time 5f winner, including at Chester; return to 6f presents a question mark

runners.name: Cherry Baker
runners.spotlight: Returns to a more realistic level on handicap debut, having faced a stiff task (well beaten) in the Nell Gwyn on reappearance; sole 6f attempt resulted in ready win at Southwell (AW) in final 2yo outing; unexposed over this distance.
runners.comment: Sole 6f attempt resulted in ready win at Southwell; unexposed over this distance

runners.name: Arduis Invicta
runners.spotlight: Campaigned at Listed level, with mixed results, since scoring off 13lb lower in last handicap attempt; peak figure may not be reliable and she needs to prove current mark is appropriate.
runners.comment: Peak figure at Listed level may not be reliable; 13lb higher than last handicap attempt

runners.name: Star Material
runners.spotlight: Best form when winning a Ripon novice event (5f, good) and second in Ayr nursery (6f, soft); chance of emerging on top in this competitive handicap partly depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance of winning partly depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Go Vince Go
runners.spotlight: Has shown no further progress since the last of his three juvenile wins and some of today's rivals are less exposed; perhaps needed his Thirsk reappearance but this looks a difficult task.
runners.comment: Has shown no further progress since the last of his three juvenile wins

runners.name: Do Bronxs
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after his winning debut last term; possibly flattered by apparent peak effort (last of five in Irish Group 1) and has something to prove off his mark in this handicap debut; gelded since last run.
runners.comment: Possibly flattered by apparent peak effort; something to prove off his mark

runners.name: Evie Ross
runners.spotlight: Has raced three times, all last August, clear best form when scoring at Chepstow (good to firm) in sole 5f attempt; something to prove on the trip front but market support should be noted.
runners.comment: Something to prove on the trip front but market support should be noted
runners.owner: S R Hope And S W Barrow

races.race_name: TMT Group Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Arabian Desert
runners.spotlight: Comes with a degree of risk having been turned over at odds of 1-8 in Pontefract maiden (1m) on seasonal debut, extending his seconditis; otherwise again sets a useful standard on his Group 3 effort at Newbury (7f, soft) last October; siblings include a 1m2f winner.
runners.comment: Sets the standard on his 2yo Group 3 effort; turned over at 1-8 in maiden on reappearance

runners.name: El Nay
runners.spotlight: Both starts on AW, close second over 1m at Lingfield then third over 7f at Kempton; again has place possibilities on form and any progress would give him a fighting chance of winning; new trip is the question mark, judged on breeding.
runners.comment: Placed in a couple of AW contests over shorter; not certain to stay 1m2f on breeding

runners.name: Tornado Tower
runners.spotlight: 850,000gns yearling purchase who is by Frankel and whose siblings include Irish Derby and St Leger winner Hurricane Lane; promising second of nine in Nottingham event (1m2f, good); may need further in due course but still warrants respect, being one who has significant potential.
runners.comment: Well bred; promising second in Nottingham event; has clear potential and warrants respect

runners.name: Outback Legend
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise with rallying sixth of nine at Nottingham (1m2f, good) but finished over 3l behind Tornado Tower and gives the impression he'll be one for handicaps later on, probably over longer trips.
runners.comment: Over 3l behind Tornado Tower at Nottingham and seems one for handicaps later on

runners.name: Norman Invasion
runners.spotlight: Gelded since 2yo runs that comprised a fifth-place finish in Ascot maiden (7f) won by high-class prospect and close third with hood fitted at Newcastle (1m, AW); headgear now removed; several of today's rivals have achieved bigger figures.
runners.comment: Gelded since his 2yo runs; several of today's rivals have achieved bigger figures

runners.name: Sottsfield
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form, having finished only eighth of 12 at Newcastle (1m, AW) in sole 2yo start; handicaps will likely be his ideal scene in due course.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form, based on his 2yo effort

races.race_name: Roodee Handicap

runners.name: Crimson Spirit
runners.spotlight: Best form at 7f on good/firmer; completed a hat-trick last summer and could be set for further success this term, having performed encouragingly (third of 18) at Newmarket on reappearance; one to consider.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly (third of 18) at Newmarket on reappearance; one to consider

runners.name: Dance In The Storm
runners.spotlight: Sometimes races too freely, including when last seen, but otherwise a progressive 7f filly and her form includes good efforts in difficult circumstances at two major festivals last August; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Sometimes races too freely but otherwise a progressive 7f filly; remains of interest

runners.name: Rare Change
runners.spotlight: Showed a resurgence in his form at Lingfield (AW) seasonal debut; did well on turf (6f/7f, good/good to firm) last May/June, winning three times, and looks interesting off a workable mark returned to this sphere.
runners.comment: Did well on turf last May/June; interesting off a workable mark back in this sphere
runners.owner: Mrs J Woods And K Lawrence

runners.name: Pietro
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive on turf and AW; prevailed at Wolverhampton (7f) on seasonal debut, making it three wins from his last six races; up only another 2lb and there's the possibility of further improvement; respected.
runners.comment: Prevailed on reappearance, making it three wins from last six races; steadily progressive

runners.name: Goldmoyne
runners.spotlight: Has built up a good strike-rate on AW for current yard; form since returned turf comprises a good effort and an excusable one; however, chance of landing this contest partly depends on the effects of new headgear.
runners.comment: Has done well for current yard but chance partly depends on the effects of new headgear

runners.name: Sujet
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who scored twice over longer trips in Ireland; far from disgraced (seventh of 18) at Newmarket dropped to 7f on stable/seasonal debut; possibilities, provided he builds on that effort.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish 4yo who was far from disgraced when dropped to 7f on stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Huscal
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-10 in handicaps but he was a good second on three occasions last term; going one better is a possibility, provided first-time tongue-tie proves beneficial with Newmarket reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: First handicap success is possible, provided first-time tongue-tie proves beneficial

runners.name: Silky Wilkie
runners.spotlight: Campaigned almost exclusively over sprint trips; consistent AW form this year includes two wins; effective on turf but hasn't scored in this sphere for three years and he's not the percentage call back at 7f.
runners.comment: Raced mainly at sprint trips; doesn't look the percentage call back over 7f
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxxxi & E Burke

runners.name: Arctic Thunder
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-9 for current stable but he has made the frame in last three attempts over 7f; could go well returned to this distance with Newbury reappearance (6f) under his belt.
runners.comment: Could go well returned to this distance with 6f reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Miami Matrix
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-9 (all last year) for Hugo Palmer; final effort took his Chester record (7.5f) to 7112; subsequently sold for 60,000gns; likely player, provided he remains in similar form on stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Final effort for Hugo Palmer took Chester record to 7112; debut for new stable

runners.name: A War Eagle
runners.spotlight: Suited by good/firmer; has quickly found his form this season for new yard and ran creditably in the Thirsk Hunt Cup (1m) last Saturday when attempting to follow up his Beverley success (7.5f); may do better still.
runners.comment: Ran creditably in Thirsk Hunt Cup last Saturday when bidding to follow up Beverley win

runners.name: Bell Shot
runners.spotlight: Did too much up front kept to AW on return from break last time, again running below the form of his January win; effective on turf but has never defied a mark as high as current one.
runners.comment: Did too much up front kept to AW last time; mark looks difficult back on turf
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Spangled Mac
runners.spotlight: Campaigned back in Bahrain since December, best efforts when placed twice over 6f; well treated on historical data but hasn't won a domestic race since 2023; returns to 7f for first time since last July.
runners.comment: Returns from another spell in Bahrain; hasn't won a domestic race since 2023

runners.name: Midnight Strike
runners.spotlight: Showed bits of useful form for Joseph O'Brien but is 0-11 since debut success; sold for 21,000gns since last run; bit to prove in this competitive scenario on stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish 4yo who is 0-11 since debut win; makes stable/seasonal debut
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

races.race_name: Kauto The King Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Etalon
runners.spotlight: Fitting of cheekpieces sparked a revival in December, when landing a fifth success over fences at Ascot (2m3f, good to soft); followed that up with a career-best RPR when third off this mark there (2m5f) the following month; respected but he'll need to leave behind a lesser effort last time.
runners.comment: Ascot win and good third since the cheekpieces went on; below par latest but respected.

runners.name: Western General
runners.spotlight: Back to form after a second wind op, posting career-best RPRs when scoring at Chepstow in February and when runner-up at Hereford the following month (both at 2m); has his best form over shorter but shapes as though this trip should suit and he commands respect on that recent form.
runners.comment: Back to form post wind op; up in trip but respected judged on recent good form.

runners.name: Somespring Special
runners.spotlight: 4-15 over fences; gained a win at Chepstow (extended 2m3f, good) in October but hit with a 7lb rise and she's been well held on both outings this year, most recently trailing home last of three at Wincanton (2m, good) three weeks ago; only 2lb lower and now has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 4-15 over fences; below par twice this year; only 2lb lower and has something to prove.

runners.name: Centara
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive over fences last summer, winning five handicaps (four here) at around 2m on ground no worse than good to soft, though he ended the campaign pulled up at Cheltenham; has since had a second wind op and could find more at this longer trip (2m3f hurdle winner), which will be needed off this mark following a break.
runners.comment: Five chase wins last summer; second wind op; steps up in trip but needs to find more.

runners.name: Light N Strike
runners.spotlight: Progressive for Emma Lavelle last term, winning this off today's mark and backing it up with solid placed efforts; however, he's struggled for this yard (early unseat in Kerry National, pulled up at Chepstow and tailed off at Cheltenham when last seen in October) and now has something to prove.
runners.comment: Won this off today's mark last year but form for this stable has been poor.

runners.name: Hercule Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: Yet to make an impact in three starts for this yard this year, but he showed some smart form in Ireland for Willie Mullins, notably landing a 2m4f Grade 3 at around this time last season; looks attractively treated on that evidence; one to watch in the market, though a return to form is needed.
runners.comment: Grade 3 winner (2m4f) for Willie Mullins last year but offered little as yet for new yard.

runners.name: Ventura Highway
runners.spotlight: Ex-French; gained a chase success at Auteuil (2m6f) last May but hasn't matched that since; current ability is a little tricky to pin down and he looks to have something to find on RPRs on this British debut.
runners.comment: Ex-French; Auteuil winner last May; below par since; bit to prove on UK debut.

races.race_name: Racing To School Reaches 25 Years Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Name Me Nicely
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden over hurdles but went very close in a Newbury 3m handicap in March last time, when may have won but for hanging late on; sets the standard back in maiden company and very much a leading contender.
runners.comment: Close 2nd in Newbury handicap last time, hanging late on; sets standard back in a maiden.

runners.name: Bold Step
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Kincardine (bumper/2m hurdle) and Steal A March (useful 2m5f-3m hurdler); made a promising debut when runner-up in an Exeter bumper in January, shaping as though this longer trip will suit now switching to hurdles; leading contender.
runners.comment: Exeter bumper runner-up; longer trip should suit now switched to hurdles; leading chance
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Lirone Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: French 2m2f hurdle winner on very soft who folded tamely at Wetherby (2m, soft; 14-1) on British debut, and fared little better when a remote fourth at Plumpton (2m4f, soft) in January; has a bit to prove returning from a break but now goes in a tongue-tie and remains in excellent hands.
runners.comment: Bit to prove returning from a break, but tongue-tie added and remains in excellent hands.

runners.name: Double Tiger
runners.spotlight: Raced freely and failed to last home over 2m4f on soft ground on hurdle debut in February after a break; showed the benefit for that run when second at Wetherby (2m, good) the following month, beaten 16l by a useful type; should be fine back up in trip and better can be expected again.
runners.comment: Much improved when 2nd at Wetherby; should do better now back up in trip again.

runners.name: Outmaster
runners.spotlight: From the family of Denman; won a 2m5f good-ground point on debut in April 2025 and subsequently sold for £90,000; absent since but makes rules debut for a leading stable.
runners.comment: Point winner in April 2025; sold for £90,000; makes rules debut for top yard.

runners.name: River Don
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt in points when winning a three-finisher maiden at Cothelstone (3m, good to soft) last month for this stable; cheekpieces go on for rules/hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Off mark at seventh attempt in points last month; cheekpieces on for rules/hurdle debut.

runners.name: Alberts Bay
runners.spotlight: Third on hurdle debut over C&D in September 2023 after a long absence, but off again afterwards and last of seven in October 2024; returns from another lengthy layoff on stable debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 9yo; career hit by absences; back after another break on this stable debut.

runners.name: Style De Folie
runners.spotlight: Beaten 53l and 31l in his two hurdle races (2m3f), with cheekpieces latest; up in trip but difficult to make a case for.
runners.comment: Well held in both hurdle starts (2m3f); cheekpieces latest; up in trip but hard to fancy.

runners.name: At Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Hinted at ability in a couple of bumpers but pulled up switched to hurdles at Hereford (2m3f, good to soft) in December following an absence and now returns from another break with something to prove.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability in bumpers but pulled up on hurdle debut; absent since.

runners.name: Jubertilee
runners.spotlight: 6-21 in points and in good heart this year, but never sighted when well held on hurdle/rules debut at Chepstow last month, possibly finding the drop to 2m counting against him; should be better suited by this stamina test but still likely to find a few too good.
runners.comment: 6-21 in points; well held Chepstow debut over 2m but this longer trip will suit.

races.race_name: No Drama This End Club Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Storminhome
runners.spotlight: Suffered a couple of heavy defeats after a long absence but then went very close to a first chase win in first-time blinkers at Southwell (3m, soft) last time, with the winner running well since; remains on a competitive mark and is one to consider if the headgear continues to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Back to form in blinkers latest and likely to play a leading role if headgear works again.

runners.name: Astronomic View
runners.spotlight: Didn't appear to stay 3m5f at Bangor two starts ago but fared little better when pulled up at Exeter last month back at a trip that should have suited; had been in good form prior to those runs and would hold at least place claims if able to rekindle that earlier spark.
runners.comment: Pulled up latest when drop in trip should have suited; earlier form gives place claims.

runners.name: Dyno Dave
runners.spotlight: Appreciated better ground when opening his chase account at the fourth attempt at Exeter (3m, good to soft) in March, but took a heavy fall back there the following month; still has more to offer in this sphere and he's respected if that latest effort hasn't dented his confidence.
runners.comment: Better ground sparked first chase win in March; heavy fall since; still open to progress.

runners.name: Elysian Knight
runners.spotlight: 0-4 over fences this term but far from disgraced on a couple of starts and ran respectably when fourth back over hurdles at Plumpton (3m1f, good) last month; yet to win in handicap company but drops in class returned to fences and warrants respect.
runners.comment: Yet to win over fences or in handicap company but needs respect dropped into a class 4.

runners.name: Rickety Bridge
runners.spotlight: Landed a Southwell maiden hurdle (2m4f, soft) in March 2024; largely out of form in limited starts since but there were signs of a revival here (3m2f, good) last month in first-time cheekpieces; needs to back that up but could improve for this switch to fences.
runners.comment: Signs of a revival last time and he could be suited by switch to fences.

races.race_name: Welcoming Newton Abbot Business Club Members Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Northern Air
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner in early 2025 but disappointing over fences in three starts for Dan Skelton, leaving questions to answer; however, got back on track over hurdles here last month when a close second (2m1f, good) and a reproduction of that back over fences would put her in the mix.
runners.comment: Yet to convince over fences but back to form reverting to hurdles on stable debut latest.

runners.name: Cobbler's Boy
runners.spotlight: Good-ground 2m maiden hurdle winner a year ago and he's looked a work in progress since switching to fences last season; latest 5l fourth at Ascot (2m1f, good) returned to better ground was encouraging though and he's come a long way down the weights; needs to build on that and not of of this if doing so.
runners.comment: Good-ground hurdle winner; yet to win over fences but latest fourth was encouraging.

runners.name: Lightning Flash
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start over fences at Chepstow (2m) in October and looked set for a second success when falling two out at Ludlow (2m) the following month; however, well held in his two runs since, with his jumping far from fluent when last of three at Doncaster in March after a break; now has something to prove.
runners.comment: Won chase debut at Chepstow but out of form since falling at Ludlow in November.

runners.name: Gower Prince
runners.spotlight: Back to near best on first start for this stable when runner-up at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) with the hood back on; remains well treated on past form after 3lb rise and holds strong claims if able to repeat the effort.
runners.comment: Back to near best when second on stable debut last time and strong claims if repeating it.

runners.name: First Angel
runners.spotlight: Lightly campaigned in recent years but a three-time chase winner for this yard, all around 2m on ground no worse than good to soft, with two of those wins under Sean Bowen; shaped as though he would improve for March's comeback after another long absence and has a history of doing just that and following up; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Three-time 2m chase winner; comeback run was likely needed after absence; not ruled out.

races.race_name: Par Inn Celebrating 50 Next Year Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Best Night
runners.spotlight: Acts on good and soft ground; C&D winner who scored at Huntingdon (2m) in March but she's taken backward steps in her two subsequent runs; only 2lb higher than for her last win but she needs to rediscover her spark.
runners.comment: Won at Huntingdon (2m, good) in March but she's taken two backward steps since

runners.name: Belle Le Grand
runners.spotlight: Won two novice hurdles on good ground last May (2m/2m1f); no impact at 12-1 or bigger in five handicaps but she rallied to finish a respectable sixth of 15 at Kelso (2m, good to soft) in March; continues to drop down the weights and could be dangerous if first-time cheekpieces work.
runners.comment: 0-5 in handicaps but she's on a reduced mark and could be dangerous with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Reel Orange
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the ninth attempt over hurdles when justifying favouritism in a C&D handicap (good; cheekpieces added) last month; there wasn't much separating the first four but a 3lb rise looks fair and her confidence should be boosted after that success; big player again in her bid to make it 2-4 for Joe Tizzard.
runners.comment: Made the breakthrough over C&D last month and she's a big player again off 3lb higher
runners.owner: J M Dare, T Hamlin, J W Snook

runners.name: Riviera Walk
runners.spotlight: Bumper scorer who won by 15l in a Catterick maiden (1m7f, good) in March but she was pulled up on her handicap debut at Kelso (2m, good to soft) last time; still has low mileage but she's not been easy to predict and needs to bounce back again.
runners.comment: Easy winner of a Catterick maiden but was pulled up on her handicap debut last time

runners.name: Future Bucks
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 5yo who won British debut at Windsor (2m, good to soft) in November but she's been disappointing since and was pulled on handicap debut at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft; 66-1) in March; that was a tough assignment but she needs a major revival on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Pulled up on handicap debut at Newbury in March and needs a major revival back in trip

runners.name: Ghost Dancing
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in both 3yo hurdle starts before Christmas but she was pulled up on handicap debut at Fakenham (2m, good to soft; 15-2) in March; in good hands and still early days but she needs a transformation after another short break.
runners.comment: Still early days but she needs a transformation after another short break

runners.name: Babychino
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo; came from long way back to finish third on handicap debut at Uttoxeter (2m, good to soft; 50-1) in March but she was beaten 11l and this is tougher from 5lb out of the weights; has work to do.
runners.comment: Third on handicap debut at Uttoxeter but she's 5lb out of the weights here; has work to do

runners.name: Miss Denver
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to three hurdle winners including the smart Polly Peachum (2m-2m6f); well held at big prices in both completed starts over hurdles but she's had only four runs and it wouldn't be a surprise if she showed a lot more on this handicap debut; needs a close look in the market.
runners.comment: Unexposed 6yo and she's a possible improver on handicap debut

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Supreme Yeats
runners.owner: The Old Stag Racing Sy Partnership

runners.name: Striking A Pose
runners.owner: S Clare G Kennington & C Knowles

runners.name: Mister Barclay
runners.owner: M Baker, K Ibberson And H Pearman

races.race_name: Strawmax Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dartmouth Castle
runners.spotlight: Sedgefield bumper winner last May (2m1f, good to firm); off the mark over hurdles with a wide-margin success on same track last time (2m4f, good; made all; left clear two out); the one to beat.
runners.comment: Bumper winner on fast ground; off the mark over hurdles last time (made all); solid claims

runners.name: Kashchei
runners.spotlight: Irish raider; exposed maiden; two fair runs on soft/heavy this year but well beaten with cheekpieces tried (on again) at Downpatrick last time (2m6f, yielding to soft); bit to prove.
runners.comment: Maiden; two fair runs on soft/heavy in the winter; well beaten latest; bit to prove

runners.name: Rupert The Prince
runners.spotlight: Won three times at up to 2m on the Flat for Paddy Butler; bought for 36,000gns in October; third in a 2m handicap at Ripon 11 days ago having disappointed in a Newcastle maiden hurdle on jumps debut in January (2m1f, good to soft; cheekpieces on instead of visor); visor back on, this longer trip should suit, and improvement likely.
runners.comment: Fair stayer on the Flat; cost 36,000gns; poor effort on jumps debut; can do better

races.race_name: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Walk On Quest
runners.spotlight: Won four handicaps on the trot two seasons ago (2m-2m5f, good to soft/soft); hasn't won since but was beaten only by a nose over C&D on good last time; contender.
runners.comment: Won 4 handicaps 2 seasons ago; sound run when beaten a nose over C&D latest; contender

runners.name: Presentandcounting
runners.spotlight: Veteran; has won nine chases, often making all in small fields on good ground; hasn't been quite at his best since Uttoxeter win last July (2m4f, good) but has plenty going for him here.
runners.comment: Nine-time winner; suited by making all in small fields on good ground so should go well

runners.name: The Clonmel Kid
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on second chase start when scoring at Newcastle last time (2m4f, good); seems to stay 3m, but this looks tougher.
runners.comment: Off the mark on second chase start last time (2m4f); seems to stay 3m; stiffer task

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Starlyte
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m5f here (good to soft) last March but he's 0-11 since and was a well-held fourth behind Kilmore Rock over C&D last month; now back on last winning mark but she's not easy to predict and others are preferred.
runners.comment: On last winning mark but she was well held behind Kilmore Rock over C&D last month

runners.name: Kilmore Rock
runners.spotlight: Won this race last year and he improved his hurdling record to 5-13 when beating Jericoacoara over C&D (second-time blinkers) two weeks ago; up 6lb but he was as good as ever with that comfortable success and is a major player again.
runners.comment: Won this last year and he was good as ever when scoring over C&D last month; key player

runners.name: Rememberthename
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the ninth attempt over hurdles when justifying favouritism in a handicap here (2m2f, good) last month; this is tougher off 5lb higher but he seems versatile trip-wise and he still has potential; respected for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism over 2m2f here last time and he's respected back up in trip

runners.name: Rexem
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in the Ulster National (3m4f, soft) in March but he was runner-up in two chase handicaps at Musselburgh (2m4f/2m7f) before that; now returns to hurdling but he's 3-8 in this sphere and is on a dangerous mark on this drop back in trip and grade; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Pulled up in Ulster National (3m4f) latest but he needs a close look back over hurdles

runners.name: Jericoacoara
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his French hurdle win (2m1f, very soft) in 2023 but he's been placed in last three runs including when second behind Kilmore Rock over C&D (good) last month; should go well again and any rain would be a bonus.
runners.comment: In-form 7yo who was runner-up over C&D last time; respected
runners.owner: G R Orchard & Mrs J M Gray

runners.name: High Dancer
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle (2m5f, good) for James Ewart last spring; runner-up in C&D handicap next time but he's not gone on from that and has been disappointing in last two runs; drops back in trip/grade but he needs to get back near his best to make an impact.
runners.comment: Disappointing in last two runs and needs to get back near his best to make an impact

races.race_name: 2000 Sacks Of Carrots Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Livano Bello
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden; creditable runs when placed at Market Rasen (2m2f, good to soft) and at Fakenham (2m4f, good) on last two starts; each-way chance.
runners.comment: 0-12; creditable runs last twice, notably when 2nd over 2m4f latest; each-way chance

runners.name: Joto
runners.spotlight: Yet to win over hurdles; third in three consecutive handicaps last season (unseated rider on final start); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Maiden; third in three consecutive handicaps last season and has an each-way chance.

runners.name: Fairly Fulling
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at 14th attempt at Newcastle in March (2m4f, good to soft); now 7lb higher; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Newcastle in March and although 7lb higher, has an each-way chance

runners.name: Disco Annie
runners.spotlight: Two wins at Carlisle in November 2024 (2m1f, soft/good to soft); mixed form since, including over fences; sound run back hurdling at Carlisle in March (2m1f, soft), but poor next time and has since had wind surgery; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Best form is at Carlisle; back after wind surgery; has a bit to prove

runners.name: Cher Tara
runners.spotlight: Won a Down Royal handicap in March 2025 for Gordon Elliott (2m4f, good); best run for present yard when third at Newcastle in January (2m4f, good to soft) but hasn't fared as well since.
runners.comment: Won for Gordon Elliott in 2025; fair third in h'cap in January, but not as good since

runners.name: Larkfield Lusive
runners.spotlight: Won handicaps on good ground at Perth (2m4f) and Market Rasen (2m7f) last summer; returns after a 263-day break;  interesting contender.
runners.comment: Two handicap wins on good last summer; back after a break; interesting contender

runners.name: Abbey Scope
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper here last April (2m, good); best run over hurdles when second to a hat-trick scorer at Catterick in January (2m3f, soft); pulled up last time (never travelled); could bounce back.
runners.comment: Bumper winner here in April 2025; pulled up over hurdles last time but could bounce back

runners.name: Lester Mail
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper here last April (2m, good); mixed form over hurdles, including fair fourth on handicap debut here last time (2m2f, good); further progress likely, particularly now up to 2m4f.
runners.comment: Bumper winner here; fair fourth over 2m2f here on h'cap debut; should fare better at 2m4f

runners.name: Nitty Gritty
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle for Joseph O'Brien last July (2m4f, yielding to soft); bit disappointing since joining Mark Walford, but still relatively unexposed.
runners.comment: 2m4f maiden hurdle win in Ireland in 2025; bit disappointing for Mark Walford so far

runners.name: She Won't Mind
runners.spotlight: Point winner in May 2023 (2m4f, good); mainly modest form over hurdles and fences; best watched having been off since last May (bled from the nose).
runners.comment: Point winner; modest form under rules; best watched having been off since last May (bled)

races.race_name: 2000 Sacks Of Carrots Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Vanilla Dancer
runners.spotlight: 1-20 over hurdles; neck second in a mares' handicap here last month (2m5f, good); each-way chance.
runners.comment: 1-20 over hurdles; neck 2nd in mares h'cap over 2m5f here last time; each-way chance

runners.name: Thorneylands
runners.spotlight: Second to a subsequent winner in a Newcastle bumper in December (2m1f, good to soft); best hurdles run when sixth in a 2m2f novice here last time (good); major contender up in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Second in a bumper; best hurdle run when 6th here latest; player up in trip on h'cap debut

runners.name: Helsgetaway
runners.spotlight: Exposed; fair fourth at Ayr in November (3m, soft) and may have needed the race after a break when well beaten at Newcastle last time (2m4f, good to soft).
runners.comment: Fair fourth at Ayr in November and may have needed the run when below par last time

runners.name: Maillot Blanc
runners.spotlight: Won a 3m Perth handicap last term (good); comes into the reckoning following two sound runs last month when third over 2m6f here (good; form sound) and when second over 3m at Perth (good).
runners.comment: 3m Perth winner last summer; sound placed runs on last two starts; very much in the mix

runners.name: Georgie's Malt
runners.spotlight: Still early days for her but she was well beaten here when stepped up to 2m5f last time (good) having shaped quite well over 2m1f on her handicap debut at Carlisle on her previous start.
runners.comment: Well beaten over 2m5f here last time having shaped well on her handicap debut over 2m1f

runners.name: Long Time Listener
runners.spotlight: Won a point bumper in February 2024 (2m, soft); has shown bits of fair form over hurdles, notably when 11.5l fourth in a Sedgefield novice handicap in March (2m4f, good); others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Won a point bumper; creditable 4th in novice h'cap hurdle in March (2m4f); others stronger

runners.name: Return To Florida
runners.spotlight: Ex-pointer; ran his best race over hurdles when fourth in a Newcastle novice last time (2m1f, good to soft); further improvement likely now up in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Best hurdles run when 4th in novice last time; should do better up in trip on h'cap debut

runners.name: Venturevango
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; pulled up in two good-ground handicaps at long odds for current yard; hood now tried; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; pulled up both runs for this yard; hood tried; best watched for now

runners.name: Lone Star
runners.spotlight: Last win came off a 5lb lower mark at Market Rasen in June last year (2m4f, good); not at her best last time but has a chance on her previous run when third at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft).
runners.comment: Last win was off 5lb lower last June; below par last time but chance on her previous run

runners.name: Royal Maggs
runners.spotlight: Ex-pointer who won a maiden on fast ground last May (3m); creditable second in a mares' maiden hurdle at Newcastle in March (2m4f, good to soft) but pulled too hard when last of four there on handicap debut last time (2m7f, good); not out of it if more relaxed this time.
runners.comment: Second in mares' novice two runs back; excuses for lesser run on h'cap debut; a possible
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing, S Knowles & Partners

races.race_name: Bedmax Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Flash Du Pistolet
runners.spotlight: Two wins over fences, one over C&D, in March 2025 (good/good to soft); ran one of his best races since when second at Hexham (1m7f, good) last time and should go well again.
runners.comment: C&D winner in March 2025; good second at Hexham last time and should go well again

runners.name: Tanking Along
runners.spotlight: Two wins at Sedgefield last season, the second a 2m1f handicap chase (good); went up 6lb for that and was put up another 7lb for finishing 4l third from 8lb out of the handicap at Ayr last month (2m, soft); could go well despite that rise.
runners.comment: Sedgefield win in March (2m1f, good); now 13lb higher after good run from 8lb wrong latest

runners.name: Chapel Green
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m5f here following a 163-day break last September (good) but rather disappointing since; on a good mark now, but a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Won over 2m5f here after a break last September; disappointing since; bit to prove now

runners.name: Singapore Trip
runners.spotlight: On a hat-trick having won for today's rider at Wetherby in March (1m7f, good) and at Market Rasen in April (2m3f, good); has gone up 10lb, but these conditions suit and he could go well again.
runners.comment: On a hat-trick after win at Wetherby and Market Rasen; conditions suit; could go well

runners.name: Intenzo
runners.spotlight: Best run over fences when 3.25l second to Tanking Along (13lb worse off) at Sedgefield in March (2m, good); reportedly ran flat when well beaten at Southwell next time (2m4f, good); should fare better.
runners.comment: Much better off with Tanking Along who beat him at Sedgefield in March; a possible

runners.name: Glory And Honour
runners.spotlight: Has won four chases, the latest off 1lb lower at Hexham last June (1m7f, good); good second following a break at Newcastle last time (2m4f, good); should go well again.
runners.comment: Four-time chase winner, latest over 1m7f last June; good second last time; should go well

runners.name: Moodofthemoment
runners.spotlight: Won a five-runner handicap at Newcastle in October (2m4f, good) but had been pulled up on previous start and was pulled up on only start since (good to soft); this ground should suit better but he's not easy to work out.
runners.comment: Won five-runner handicap at Newcastle in October (good); pulled up only start since

races.race_name: Aidan & Margaret McDonnell's Golden Anniversary Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Whatdeawant
runners.spotlight: Ex-Willie Mullins; smart novice hurdler, third in the 2022 Ballymore at Cheltenham; has won four of his last five points (all for today's rider), and was runner-up at Hexham on hunter chase debut last time (3m, good; Settle Down Jill third, Brandy McQueen and Dressedtoimpress both pulled up); the one to beat.
runners.comment: Has won 4 of last five points and a good 2nd on hunter chase debut; the one to beat

runners.name: Northern Bound
runners.spotlight: Won two 2m4f handicaps for Ben Pauling last year, the second a two-runner event at Warwick; bit to prove after being pulled up  on hunter chase debut at Ludlow in March (2m4f, good), including stamina.
runners.comment: Two 2m4f h'cap wins for Ben Pauling in 2025; pulled up on stable debut; stamina to prove

runners.name: Settle Down Jill
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner for Dan Skelton; placed on his last two runs in hunter chases, last time finishing third at Hexham (3m, good) where Whatdeawant was 18l in front of her in second; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Fair form in hunter chases but held by Whatdeawant on last month's Hexham form

runners.name: Brandy McQueen
runners.spotlight: Won a similar event at Catterick in good style in March (3m1f, good) but pulled up at Hexham last time; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Won similar event at Catterick in March, but pulled up next time; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Royal Cave
runners.spotlight: Maiden point winner in March (3m, soft), but a 20-race maiden under rules; last of two finishers at Pusk on latest point start; stiffer task here.
runners.comment: Maiden point winner but 0-20 under rules; last of two on latest point start; stiff task

runners.name: Dressedtoimpress
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 10yo; won the Heart Of All England (maiden hunter chase) at Hexham last May (3m, good); pulled up over same C&D last month on only run since; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Won maiden hunter chase at Hexham last May; pulled up on only run since; bit to prove

runners.name: Eagles Rock
runners.spotlight: Five-time point winner, latest at Overton last month (3m, good); creditable second in maiden hunter chase over C&D last April only previous rules run; interesting.
runners.comment: Five-time point winner, latest in March; 2nd over C&D only previous rules run; interesting

races.race_name: Racing TV Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Category 3 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Datstheholyallofit
runners.spotlight: Similar level of form when fourth in two Sedgefield bumpers (2m1f, good); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Similar level of form when fourth in two Sedgefield bumpers (good); each-way chance

runners.name: Allroadsleadtorome
runners.spotlight: By Golden Horn; gelded since finishing fifth of six in a Naas bumper in February (1m7f, heavy; 20-1); very different ground this time, cheekpieces tried and interesting down in grade.
runners.comment: Gelded since 5th of six at Naas in February; headgear tried; interesting down in grade

runners.name: Silk Buds
runners.spotlight: Irish raider; pulled up in a mares' maiden point in April (3m, yielding to soft; 5-1); should benefit from the experience and could be a player.
runners.comment: Irish raider; pulled up in a point only run so far; should improve; quite interesting

runners.name: Pony Express
runners.spotlight: Home-bred by Frontiersman out of a staying-bred mare who was well beaten in a bumper; wears blinkers on debut.
runners.comment: Home-bred by Frontiersman; dam unplaced in a bumper; blinkered on debut.

runners.name: Office Banter
runners.spotlight: 10,000euros 3yo by Schiaparelli out of a useful 2m4f-3m chase winner; half-brother to a 2m hurdle winner; interesting newcomer from a yard with a good record in bumpers.
runners.comment: 10,000euros 3yo; out of a useful 2m4f-3m chase winner; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Furdy
runners.spotlight: Home-bred by Havana Grey out of a 7f-8.5f winner; bred more for the Flat and not an obvious candidate.
runners.comment: Home-bred by Havana Grey out of a 7f-8.5f winner; not an obvious bumper candidate

runners.name: Mortgage Man
runners.spotlight: 2,000euros 3yo by Mahler; dam third in a point; looks trainer's second string.
runners.comment: 2,000euros 3yo by Mahler; dam was third in a point; looks trainer's second string

runners.name: Smart Order
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way when tailed-off last of seven on debut at Sedgefield last month (2m1f, good; 11-1); significant improvement needed.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on last month's Sedgefield debut; significant improvement needed

runners.name: Teescomponents Gal
runners.spotlight: Mare by Jack Hobbs; weakened late on when tailed off in a C&D bumper last month (good; 50-1); should improve for the experience.
runners.comment: Tailed off on last month's debut when 50-1 for a C&D bumper but should come on for the run

runners.name: Western Games
runners.spotlight: Mare by Westerner; beaten 26l when sixth of seven on debut at Hexham last month (2m, good; 22-1); should do better.
runners.comment: Beaten 26l when sixth of seven at Hexham on debut last month; should do better

races.race_name: Unibet Supports Safer Gambling "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Part Of Racing Excellence)

runners.name: King Of Speed
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a win at Wolverhampton (8.5f) in December; has had some ups and downs since but he came from off the pace to finish a creditable third at Lingfield (1m, Polytrack) two weeks ago; now 3lb lower than for his last success and has claims if he can build on that last run.
runners.comment: Multiple AW winner who was third at Lingfield latest and has claims if he can back that up

runners.name: Sceptic
runners.spotlight: Triple AW winner who showed signs of a revival at Wolverhampton in March and he went close back on turf at Nottingham (8.3f, good) last month; 2lb higher here but he was a well-backed favourite last time and he remains very well treated on old form; key player.
runners.comment: Resurgent 6yo who went close at Nottingham (8.3f) last month; big player back on AW

runners.name: Dinah Myte
runners.spotlight: Won at Ffos Las (1m, heavy) last autumn but she's been well below that form in three runs this year; record is now 1-11 and she needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Well below form form in her three runs this year and overall record is now 1-11

runners.name: Port Noir
runners.spotlight: 9yo who has won four times since February including twice over C&D; held in third when unpenalised at Wolverhampton last time but she wasn't beaten far and that was another creditable effort; should be in the mix again off 3lb higher.
runners.comment: Four wins since February and was a good third at Wolverhampton latest; respected up 3lb

runners.name: Aim For The Bull
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough at Southwell (7f classified) in February and he got back on the scoresheet in a C&D handicap in March; never dangerous when fourth at Southwell last time but this step back up in trip looks a positive and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Won over C&D on his penultimate run and he's in the mix back up in trip

runners.name: Miss Chester
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f maiden here last May but she's 0-8 since and has form figures of 8000879 in handicaps; continues to drop down the weights but she needs a major revival.
runners.comment: Won a maiden here last May but she has form figures of 8000879 in handicaps

runners.name: Daddy Chill
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo but he's generally struggled so far and was tailed off in a C&D handicap (40-1) in March; dropped another 5lb but he's with another new yard now and has plenty to prove; first-time hood replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but he's generally struggled so far; has a lot to prove for another new yard

runners.name: Berning Hot
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with a 33-1 third over C&D in January but she's been well held in both subsequent runs (1m/7f); has bit to prove again and her overall record is now 1-16.
runners.comment: Record of 1-16 and she's been well held on AW (1m/7f) in last two runs

runners.name: Marchetti
runners.spotlight: Has lots of Kempton form but she's been out of sorts over C&D in last three runs and her last win was two years ago; opposable.
runners.comment: Her last win was two years ago and she's been out of sorts over C&D in last three runs

races.race_name: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Booziebrunch
runners.spotlight: Blinkers helped last summer and they're on for the first time this season, after two fair efforts; more exposed than the rest of these but 1m on Polytrack is ideal and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Blinkers helped last summer and they're back on; 1m on Polytrack is ideal; one to consider

runners.name: Blues And Royals
runners.spotlight: Solid effort to justify favouritism on his C&D comeback in March, having been gelded, when held up off an ordinary pace; backed that up when one of three to come away behind a cannily ridden winner on turf latest and holds leading claims upped 2lb.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism on his C&D comeback in March; solid second since; shortlisted
runners.owner: D Fish, S Jones & J Barnett

runners.name: Victory Ace
runners.spotlight: Picked up from Roger Varian for 40,000gns last autumn and had excuses on debut for his current yard (then gelded); easy to back, travelled well and got there in the nick of time on his 7f Lingfield comeback; improvement left upped 3lb.
runners.comment: Travelled well and got there in the nick of time on his 7f comeback; improvement left

runners.name: Trio
runners.spotlight: Fair comeback at Bath a few weeks ago, when having two of tonight's rivals well in rear; entitled to have come on for that first start in more than five months.
runners.comment: Fair comeback at Bath a few weeks ago; entitled to have come on for that; each-way shout
runners.owner: M H Dixon & J L Rowsell

runners.name: Crafty Blue
runners.spotlight: Confirmed debut promise when winning a 7f Tapeta novice last autumn; well backed ahead of her Bath comeback on rain-softened ground but finished a long way back after fluffing the start; bit to prove after that.
runners.comment: Backed ahead comeback on rain-softened ground but finished a long way back; bit to prove

runners.name: Melody De Vega
runners.spotlight: Maiden who made a lot of the running on her C&D comeback in late March off the same mark; that came in a small field against her own sex and this looks more competitive, so she'll need to step up.
runners.comment: Made a lot of the running on her C&D comeback; this looks more competitive
runners.owner: Michael P Tudor & J P M O'Connor

runners.name: High Approval
runners.spotlight: 400,000euros breeze-up buy who proved expensive to follow in maiden/novice company over 6f last year; just a fair comeback at Newmarket three weeks ago, upped to 7f on on his handicap debut, and needs improvement for today's extra furlong.
runners.comment: Costly to follow; just a fair comeback and needs improvement for today's extra furlong

runners.name: Chapman's Peak
runners.spotlight: Won two nurseries here last autumn (C&D, 7f), latterly off a 4lb lower mark; made a low-key comeback on rain-softened turf recently (Trio third; Crafty Blue behind) but should fare better back on Polytrack.
runners.comment: Low-key turf comeback (Trio third; Crafty Blue behind); should do better back on Polytrack

runners.name: Bennyworth
runners.spotlight: Nothing exciting in two runs for James Owen, or on his debut for this yard in February; offered more on his handicap debut (same mark) having been gelded, behind a highly progressive winner last week, and that gave something to build on.
runners.comment: Offered more on his handicap debut last week, and that gave something to build on

races.race_name: Get Best Odds Guaranteed At Unibet Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Brilliant Star
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to 1m5f winner Star Of Light out of the yard's three-time Group 1 winner (including Irish Oaks) Star Catcher; needed the experience in one start (1m, Tapeta) last autumn before easily winning a Yarmouth novice on comeback; plenty of potential.
runners.comment: Won a Yarmouth novice easily on comeback; plenty of potential and should defy the penalty

runners.name: Golden Step
runners.spotlight: May-foaled sister to Alfred Spero, who won an ordinary 1m3f Tapeta handicap earlier this year; it will be interesting to see how she goes in the market.
runners.comment: Sister to an AW winner; it will be interesting to see how she goes in the market

runners.name: Blue Birdie
runners.spotlight: From a decent family (half-sister to a dual middle-distance Listed winner in Ireland); yard not known for winning newcomers and she's likely best watched.
runners.comment: From a decent family; yard not known for winning newcomers and she's likely best watched

runners.name: Joanna Hiffernan
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to useful handicapper (1m-2m) Alfred Boucher, who improved with time; no surprise were staying handicaps to be her thing down the line too.
runners.comment: Half-sister Alfred Boucher; no surprise were staying handicaps to be her thing too

runners.name: Eloquencia
runners.spotlight: Big price and finished well off the front two on her small-field Newmarket debut (7f) three weeks ago; goes right up in trip tonight (stamina not assured on the dam's side) and needs plenty of improvement.
runners.comment: Goes right up in trip tonight (stamina not assured on the dam's side) and needs a lot more

runners.name: Emerald Reine
runners.spotlight: 250-1 for her Yarmouth debut last month, when last of seven; can't be considered.
runners.comment: 250-1 for her Yarmouth debut last month, when last of seven; can't be considered

runners.name: Shirakawa
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners in France (including Group 3) who finished well held on her 1m debut here in January; better effort over C&D five weeks ago but she may find life easier once handicapping after tonight.
runners.comment: Better effort over C&D latest; may find life easier once handicapping after tonight

runners.name: Dream Of Ithaca
runners.spotlight: Long way off the front pair on her comeback in a 0-60 Brighton handicap a fortnight ago; goes up in trip again on her AW debut and will find this somewhat tougher.
runners.comment: Long way off the front pair on her comeback in a 0-60 Brighton handicap a fortnight ago

races.race_name: Unibet More Extra Place Races Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Venetian Gold
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns 2yo who shaped nicely behind three with prior experience when among the favourites on her Doncaster debut 11 days ago; signs of greenness that day, following a slow start, and this looks weaker if anything; respected.
runners.comment: Shaped nicely on her debut; signs of greenness that day and this looks weaker; respected

runners.name: Cixi
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to two US dirt winners; wasn't disgraced in two runs at Meydan around the turn of the year, not looking to see out 1m latterly; this is less competitive back from a break.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in two runs at Meydan around the turn of the year; this is less competitive

runners.name: Iqbaal
runners.spotlight: First foal of a once-raced half-sister to two winners, one over 5f, the other at up to 1m4f; stable yet to get rolling for the year; market will provide some clues.
runners.comment: Stable yet to get rolling for the year; market will provide some clues

runners.name: Perdy In Paris
runners.spotlight: Outran market expectations on her 6f debut last October before pulling too hard upped to this trip a fortnight later; yard ended a losing run recently.
runners.comment: Outran market expectations on her 6f debut last October; yard ended a losing run recently

runners.name: Elshie
runners.spotlight: Single-figure price for her recent turf debut (6f), when showing signs of inexperience and rather drawn away from the action; entitled to improve in the first-time hood.
runners.comment: Signs of inexperience and rather drawn away from the action on debut; entitled to improve

runners.name: Ronstadt
runners.spotlight: 70,000euros yearling who's out of an unraced half-sister to numerous winners; likely she's going to want more of a test in due course on pedigree and stall 14 isn't ideal for a newcomer; market useful.
runners.comment: Likely to want more of a test in due course on pedigree; stall 14 not ideal; market useful
runners.owner: Mrs Bv Sangster,Justin Casse & Dr J Berk

runners.name: Sterling Fortune
runners.spotlight: 39,000gns yearling who's a half-sister to eight winners, notably Sterling Knight and Charmed (Polytrack); doubt she'd need to be anything out of the ordinary on debut and the market will be the ultimate guide.
runners.comment: 39,000gns yearling who's a half-sister to eight winners; market will be the ultimate guide

runners.name: Sierra Sue
runners.spotlight: Speed in her pedigree and she cracked late over a stiff 8.5f on her turf debut three weeks ago; that wasn't a bad effort and she's entitled to improve down in distance.
runners.comment: Speed in her pedigree; not a bad debut and she's entitled to improve down in distance

runners.name: Katie's Song
runners.spotlight: Second foal of an ordinary 1m4f winner; caught the eye at a big price on her recent 7f Tapeta debut; worth keeping tabs on in the market but this easier 7f from a wide stall doesn't look optimal and handicaps may be her time to shine.
runners.comment: Caught the eye at a big price on her recent 7f Tapeta debut; market will reveal plenty

runners.name: Komodo Rose
runners.spotlight: Fair debut for Alice Haynes in an ordinary C&D fillies' maiden last August and ran to a similar level behind a short-priced favourite in one more start; market useful for her new yard back from eight months off.
runners.comment: Ran to a similar level in two starts for Alice Haynes; market useful for new yard

runners.name: Coxy's Star
runners.spotlight: Absent for nine months since finishing a remote last of 12 at Windsor last July; likely best watched uness there's good support this time.
runners.comment: Absent for nine months since finishing a remote last of 12 at Windsor last July

runners.name: Snow Day
runners.spotlight: Huge prices for two runs last autumn, finishing tailed off each time; can't be backed.
runners.comment: Huge prices for two runs last autumn, finishing tailed off each time; can't be backed

runners.name: Lady Of Chivalry
runners.spotlight: Inclined to hang right on her Wolverhampton debut in an ordinary maiden last summer and has since left Andrew Balding; drawn wider than ideal in a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Left Andrew Balding after one run; drawn wider than ideal in a first-time tongue-tie

runners.name: Starborn
runners.spotlight: Easy to back, finished well held in one run over 6f on turf last July; would want to see support this time before considering her after ten months off.
runners.comment: Easy to back, finished well held in one run over 6f on turf last July; off ten months

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap

runners.name: Mr Baloo
runners.spotlight: Added to a good record in the spring when winning off 2lb lower at Lingfield (1m) last month in first-time cheekpieces; a long way below that level on turf since and this trip is a bare minimum round here, especially from a wide stall.
runners.comment: Good record in the spring; this trip is a bare minimum, especially from a wide stall

runners.name: Farasi Lane
runners.spotlight: Two wins and a near-miss to his name in eight starts here and boasts a decent record in the spring down the years, added to when he went in twice at Wolverhampton in March; hit traffic last time and the harder they go the better for him.
runners.comment: Two wins and a near-miss to his name in eight starts here; the harder they go the better

runners.name: Ribenska
runners.spotlight: Entitled to have come on for her comeback Southwell second to a progressive 3yo three weeks ago; the evidence points to her being happier on the AW than turf and she's relatively low mileage still; yard is in decent form; one to consider.
runners.comment: Seems happier on the AW than turf and she's relatively low mileage still; one to consider

runners.name: I'm Workin On It
runners.spotlight: C&D regular who's progressed well since handicapping, with three wins here, latterly off 8lb lower in February; signs the last twice that he may have reached his ceiling off this mark and he needs to find more for last year's successful yard.
runners.comment: C&D regular who's progressed well since handicapping; needs to find more off this mark

runners.name: Receipt
runners.spotlight: Again inclined to hang right but won easily on his handicap debut last week (Tapeta) and is the least exposed of these; this is more competitive under a penalty but he has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: Again inclined to hang right but won easily on handicap debut last week (Tapeta); player

runners.name: Silca Bay
runners.spotlight: Done well since cheekpieces went on, winning three round here last autumn, latterly off 4lb lower; solid comeback behind Mr Baloo a month ago but has to prove this mark within range and stall 14 isn't ideal for a prominent-racer.
runners.comment: Done well since cheekpieces went on; has to prove this mark within range; draw not ideal

runners.name: Iconic Times
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Heather Main came over 6f and he hasn't always convinced over this trip; never involved for his new yard last time, when trying 1m for the first time, and needs much more into a stronger handicap.
runners.comment: Never involved for new yard latest, when trying 1m for the first time, and needs much more

runners.name: Arctician
runners.spotlight: Five-time C&D winner who's not well drawn for one happiest on the sharp end; resumed in good form, with two solid efforts over 1m, but has never won off a mark this high and isn't improving at his age.
runners.comment: Five-time C&D winner; not well drawn for one happiest on sharp end; high in the weights

runners.name: Tea Sea
runners.spotlight: Four-time Tapeta winner who resumes from eight months off on a career-high mark; gone well fresh before but is used to plying his trade in lesser company and may need some help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Resumes from eight months off on a career-high mark; gone well fresh before though

runners.name: Notimeforchitchat
runners.spotlight: Three wins from nine Kempton starts, including over C&D last spring on his comeback; returns from five months off close to his last successful mark and there are positives, with the usual headgear in place.
runners.comment: Three wins from nine Kempton starts; there are positives, with the usual headgear in place

runners.name: Prosperitas
runners.spotlight: Two wins came on heavy ground in the autumn; pulled hard and didn't see it out on his first crack at 1m on comeback but has to prove this career-high mark within range on his AW debut.
runners.comment: Didn't see it out on his first try at 1m on comeback; has to prove this mark within range

runners.name: Zero Carbon
runners.spotlight: Four wins from ten starts over C&D but looked on the downgrade during a limited campaign last year and turned in an ordinary comeback here two months ago; some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Four wins from ten starts over C&D; on the downgrade during a limited campaign last year

runners.name: Kit Gabriel
runners.spotlight: Made all when making a winning debut for this yard over C&D last autumn (all four victories come late in the year); back on a workable mark and back in form lately but this is more competitive.
runners.comment: Back on a workable mark and back in form lately but this is more competitive

runners.name: Blue Prince
runners.spotlight: Only won one of his last 25 starts and regressed last year; now 11lb lower than when beaten a nose over C&D last spring but there's nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Only won one of last 25 starts and regressed last year; others have more pressing claims

races.race_name: Get Daily Price Boosts At Unibet Handicap

runners.name: Future Cutlet
