runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who regressed for this yard last year and has landed the outside stall; however, didn't look to see out an extended 1m on his turf comeback and is on a competitive mark now in first-time headgear; possibilities.
runners.comment: Regressed for this yard last year but on a competitive mark now in first-time headgear

runners.name: Yehudi
runners.spotlight: Consistent maiden who's 3lb lower than when beaten a neck over C&D in February; continued in form since, the last twice for an inexperienced 7lb claimer, and is one to consider.
runners.comment: 3lb lower than when beaten a neck over C&D in February; continued in form since; player

runners.name: Taskheer
runners.spotlight: Three-time Polytrack winner, twice in a hood, who goes in first-time cheekpieces; has been enjoying a consistent spell, albeit at the bottom level.
runners.comment: Enjoying a consistent spell, albeit at the bottom level

runners.name: Luminous Warrior
runners.spotlight: 1m winner here in the winter off 2lb lower who's struggled badly in three runs since (possible excuses for two of them); stable form could be better and he has to bounce back.
runners.comment: 1m winner here in the winter off 2lb lower who's struggled badly since; has to bounce back

runners.name: Bint Havana Gold
runners.spotlight: Not as consistent now but goes well here and she's capable of popping up if the switch from cheekpieces to first-time blinkers helps; pulled hard back up to this trip last time, following a spell sprinting, and will need to settle better.
runners.comment: Not as consistent now but goes well here and she's capable of popping up if settling
runners.owner: Alan & Christine Bright And John Henwood

runners.name: Orbital Chime
runners.spotlight: Has been threatening for his current yard this spring but a couple of expensive defeats latterly; goes back to the visor from blinkers and, while capable off this mark, his strike-rate doesn't inspire.
runners.comment: Couple of expensive defeats lately; capable off this mark but strike-rate doesn't inspire

runners.name: Divine Wish
runners.spotlight: C&D fourth in January, in a maiden not run to suit, reads well enough; blew the start here when last seen five weeks ago and begins handicap life off a sensible mark; one to consider.
runners.comment: C&D fourth in January, in a maiden not run to suit, reads well enough; one to consider

runners.name: Noble Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Poor, regressive maiden who's barely beaten a rival in half a dozen runs for this yard; the first-time eyshield/blinkers will need to make quite a difference.
runners.comment: Poor, regressive maiden who's barely beaten a rival in half a dozen runs for this yard

runners.name: Bonnita
runners.spotlight: Well suited by soft/heavy ground on turf, as was her sister Queen's Eyot; off since a below-par effort last October and is some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Off since a below-par effort last October and is some way down the pecking order
runners.owner: G E Bassett & P R Chamings

runners.name: Ajrad
runners.spotlight: Most wins come later in the year and is high in the weights (13lb higher than when last successful on the AW) after three turf victories last summer; not been at his best in two runs back.
runners.comment: High in the weights currently; not been at his best in two runs back

runners.name: Withoutfurtherado
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden in Ireland (5f-1m) who ran well off this mark at York last July; makes his AW debut for a new yard back from an absence and would want to see some market confidence.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; makes his AW debut for a new yard back from an absence; market useful

runners.name: Monsieur Patat
runners.spotlight: Up in the weights after two turf wins last year and has never won off a mark this high on the AW; finished the season with a whimper and may need this at his age back from six months off.
runners.comment: Finished last season with a whimper and may need this at his age back from six months off

races.race_name: Unibet's 3 Uniboosts A Day Handicap

runners.name: Hint Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 7f (including on Polytrack) in February and he went close off this mark at Southwell last Tuesday; usually races prominently and he's strongly respected on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Two AW wins in February and he went close at Southwell last week; respected back in trip

runners.name: Court Of Session
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner who scored at Wolverhampton (7f) in January but he's been out of sorts on Tapeta in his last two runs; has plenty to prove again and all of his wins have been over longer trips.
runners.comment: All of his wins have been over longer trips and he's been out of sorts in last two runs

runners.name: Dancing With Drums
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in handicaps for Martin Dunne, winning at Southwell and at Lingfield (both over 6f) towards the end of last summer; only 1lb higher than for his last success but he hasn't threatened in two runs for current yard and needs to rediscover his spark.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo but he needs to rediscover his spark back in trip

runners.name: Respectable Jack
runners.spotlight: Went close over C&D in January but she was disappointing when joint-favourite at Chelmsford the following month; needs to step up again after that and she's now 0-13; first-time visor replaces blinkers.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden who was disappointing at Chelmsford (6f) last time; first-time visor

runners.name: Rye
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden but she's returned from a break with three places efforts this spring and first two were over C&D; her mark hasn't moved much and has each-way claims again back at this track; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 0-15 but she's been placed in last three runs including twice over C&D; in the mix

runners.name: Unico
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than when winning at Brighton (5f, soft) last September but she's not easy to predict and was last of eight at Wolverhampton in December; has bit to prove after that and this is competitive on her return.
runners.comment: On workable mark but she's not easy to predict and this is competitive on her return

runners.name: Big Bard
runners.spotlight: Turned things around with a win over C&D (17-2) in January but he's back in the doldrums in two subsequent runs at this track; comes with risks attached and he needs another bolt from the blue.
runners.comment: Bounced back with a win over C&D in January but he's struggled in both subsequent runs

runners.name: Elvetham
runners.spotlight: Has a patchy profile but he's won two of his last four starts including a comfortable success at Lingfield (6f; blinkers added) last Thursday; has a penalty but he was as good as ever last time and he's still unexposed at this trip; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Has won two of his last four including at Lingfield last week; respected under a penalty

runners.name: Man On A Mission
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who recorded his eighth AW win when coming from last to first at Southwell (5f) last week; 5lb higher under a penalty but he remains feasibly treated and is a big player again back up in trip.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who won at Southwell (5f) last week; big player again under a penalty

runners.name: Fletcher's Flight
runners.spotlight: Four-time turf winner but he's 0-6 on AW and finished down the field at Chelmsford (6f) and Southwell (5f) in final two runs for Charlie McBride; changed hands for 6,000gns and he has something to prove for new yard after 166 days off; blinkers removed.
runners.comment: All four wins have been on turf and he has something to prove on return for new yard

runners.name: Due Date
runners.spotlight: Four wins in a purple patch in the second half of 2025; now 5lb lower than for his last success but he's struggled at big prices in five runs this year and needs to turn things around; one of two runners for the yard.
runners.comment: On reduced mark but he's struggled at big prices in five runs this year
runners.owner: A Bangs & Miss J A Challen

runners.name: Lethal Blast
runners.spotlight: Won four times over 5f in 2021 but her last run was four years ago and has a lot to prove for new yard after a mammoth absence.
runners.comment: Returns after four years off and has a lot to prove for new yard

races.race_name: KSR Lighting, The Ultimate Lighting Solutions Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Miss Goldfire
runners.spotlight: Two wins last spring on good ground included a 2m7f handicap off this very mark; clearly a contender if back to that level, though that's not been the case in her last four races.
runners.comment: Good mark on last year's winning form but she has rather lost her way
runners.owner: Harry Fry Racing Syndicate Miss Goldfire

runners.name: Bradman
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back 2m3f wins here during the winter (good to soft/soft) but nothing like so effective in his last two races.
runners.comment: Two wins here during the winter but nothing like so effective in his last two races

runners.name: Washington Singer
runners.spotlight: Hadn't always looked straightforward so the new cheekpieces were probably a factor in his narrow defeat of an in-form rival at Plumpton (2m4f, good); still nicely treated off 3lb higher.
runners.comment: Beat an in-form rival at Plumpton so still nicely treated off 3lb higher

runners.name: Jamaicaine
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick in handicap hurdles at about 2m3f (good to soft/soft) before finishing tailed off on her chase debut two months ago; even if back on song returned to hurdles, she's 10lb higher than for last win which was gained only narrowly.
runners.comment: Tailed off on chase debut latest; returns to hurdles but might be on a tough mark now

runners.name: Marhaba Million
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat winner so he's been disappointing over hurdles, though has finished within 10l of the winner in his last two handicaps (2m4f/3m); a visor replaces cheekpieces and this aid did nothing for him on the Flat.
runners.comment: Useful Flat winner so he's been disappointing over hurdles; visor added

runners.name: Royal Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2023 and he's been off the track since pulled up in 3m handicap in November; on a useful mark if resurgent after a break.
runners.comment: Winless since 2023 but on a useful mark if resurgent after a break

runners.name: Mistress Fox
runners.spotlight: Some light at the end of the tunnel after a 13l defeat here in March (2m1f, good to soft) and this longer distance should suit as she's got a stout pedigree; quite interesting with that in mind.
runners.comment: Better run last time out and she's bred for this longer distance

runners.name: Herbygoesbananas
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; 6-1 for handicap debut over 2m3f here four weeks ago and trailed the winner by 19l; only 2lb lower now.
runners.comment: 6-1 for handicap debut over 2m3f here four weeks ago and trailed the winner by 19l

runners.name: Hillsin
runners.spotlight: Won in a small field at Exeter (2m2f, good to firm) in October but holding his form is becoming troublesome; risky enough.
runners.comment: Exeter winner in October but holding his form is becoming troublesome

runners.name: Bread And Butter
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has been out of sorts for a while now and he's looking sharply regressive.
runners.comment: Veteran who has been out of sorts for a while now and he's looking sharply regressive

races.race_name: Mercedes-Benz Of Chichester Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Flash Man
runners.spotlight: Two-time French Flat winner; getting it together over hurdles and penalised for beating a talented but frustrating horse of Dan Skelton's at Plumpton (2m, good); should have every chance.
runners.comment: Penalised for beating a talented but frustrating horse of Dan Skelton's at Plumpton

runners.name: La Cadalora
runners.spotlight: Fair stayer on the Flat for James Fanshawe; in this new headgear combination, she won comfortably at Wincanton (1m7f, good) and should progress some more.
runners.comment: Won comfortably at Wincanton (1m7f, good) and should progress some more

runners.name: Mega Etoile
runners.spotlight: Latest fourth at Stratford (good) was a step in the right direction but she'll need to step up again, and probably by some margin.
runners.comment: Latest fourth at Stratford (good) was a step in the right direction; more needed

runners.name: Cockney Cleopatra
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced mare; beaten only 6l in a Clonmel maiden on final run in Ireland; let down by her jumping on stable debut here four weeks ago (28-1).
runners.comment: One good run in Ireland; let down by her jumping on stable debut here four weeks ago (28s)

runners.name: Tetsworth
runners.spotlight: Showed ability on the Flat without winning; faced a stiff task on hurdle debut and was still in contention when unseating at Plumpton in a maiden won by Flash Man; only 11-2 that day and could have a role to play in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Still in contention when unseating at Plumpton in a maiden won by Flash Man

runners.name: Bahati Bahati
runners.spotlight: Third of 12 and beaten about 13l in a Ffos Las novice last month (good to soft); that was better but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Improved third of 12 last time out but others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Moscow Star
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably in two of his three bumpers, though only recorded RPRs in the high 80s; best watched on this switch to hurdles.
runners.comment: Ran respectably in two of his three bumpers, though only recorded RPRs in the high 80s

runners.name: Chambers
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden on the Flat and ended up with a low handicap mark; he'd be a surprise winner on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden on the Flat and ended up with a low handicap mark

runners.name: Regal Icon
runners.spotlight: Mildly encouraging bumper debut but very opposable on subsequent exploits.
runners.comment: Mildly encouraging bumper debut but very opposable on subsequent exploits

runners.name: Pivotal Days
runners.spotlight: 7f winner who joined this yard for 7,500gns; finished 22l behind La Cadalora on hurdle debut at Wincanton.
runners.comment: 7f winner; finished 22l behind La Cadalora on hurdle debut at Wincanton

runners.name: Oblemenko
runners.spotlight: A long way behind Flash Man at Plumpton in April and was no better next time; handicaps will be more his thing.
runners.comment: A long way behind Flash Man at Plumpton in April and was no better next time

runners.name: Hollysaway
runners.spotlight: 200-1 when tailed off in a novice hurdle for mares at Chepstow in March.
runners.comment: 200-1 when tailed off in a novice hurdle for mares at Chepstow in March

runners.name: Hawa Jumeirah
runners.spotlight: Won twice on the Flat but nothing positive to take out of two hurdle runs for this yard.
runners.comment: Won twice on the Flat but nothing positive to take out of two hurdle runs for this yard

runners.name: Dodging The Bullet
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in all three runs over hurdles; also a maiden on the Flat, though was runner-up in a 1m3f AW handicap on final run in March; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Tailed off in all runs over hurdles; also a maiden on the Flat, though ran well last time

races.race_name: KSR Lighting Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Valirann Gold
runners.spotlight: Course winner; a lot of his form is over shorter but he proved his stamina for 3m when runner-up at Ffos Las two weeks ago (good); unlikely to be far away off the same mark.
runners.comment: Proved his stamina for 3m when runner-up at Ffos Las two weeks ago

runners.name: Gata Ban
runners.spotlight: Hasn't kicked on from a second on chase debut but did some good late work behind Boys Of Wexford at Taunton the last time; that was over 2m7f and now goes further.
runners.comment: Did some good late work behind Boys Of Wexford at Taunton the last time

runners.name: Ballyfinn
runners.spotlight: Lengthy breaks are a feature in his profile; beaten at least 10l in all four runs over fences (2m5f-3m1f) and brought up the rear last time at Plumpton; unconvincing.
runners.comment: Beaten at least 10l in all four runs over fences (2m5f-3m1f)

runners.name: Land Girl's Luck
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner; fourth of ten on chase debut at Exeter where 2m3f was on the sharp side; should run well.
runners.comment: Fourth of ten on chase debut at Exeter where 2m3f was on the sharp side

runners.name: Frankie Faulkner
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner; 0-11 over fences but placed a good few times and his backward step last time was over hurdles; cheekpieces return with a tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: 0-11 over fences but placed a few times and his backward step last time was over hurdles

runners.name: Anytrixwilldo
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles and 1-14 as a chaser; clearly difficult to win with but arrives here in a good run of form and always a chance that new headgear might give him a lift; an assured stayer.
runners.comment: Arrives in a good run of form and always a chance that new headgear might give him a lift

runners.name: Boys Of Wexford
runners.spotlight: Returned to winning ways in the new visor at Taunton (2m7f, good) but whether he'll follow one good run with another is questionable and he's gone up 6lb.
runners.comment: Returned to winning ways in the new visor at Taunton; no good thing to repeat that form

runners.name: Tractor Jack
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced hurdler; jumping was a bit iffy on chase debut at Newton Abbot (2m5f) but could be wiser for that experience, and he wasn't beaten all that far despite finishing last.
runners.comment: He wasn't beaten all that far despite finishing last on chase debut last time

runners.name: Drumheller
runners.spotlight: Not solid on his hurdling exploits (0-7) and he ran no race on chase debut last time (2m4f); this longer trip is probably a better fit for him so may leave that latest run behind.
runners.comment: Not solid on his hurdling exploits (0-7) and ran no race on chase debut last time (2m4f)

runners.name: Stadmallen
runners.spotlight: This season's pointing form is uninspiring; 10lb out of the weights for recent yard debut at Ffos Las but was only 12-1 and he fell mid-race; only 2lb wrong this time and could be a lively contender.
runners.comment: Fell mid-race on recent yard debut; was only 12-1 despite being 10lb out of the weights

runners.name: Friends Don't Ask
runners.spotlight: Finished first at Southwell (3m2f, good; later disqualified) in July 2024 but has pulled up in both runs following a long absence.
runners.comment: Clear risks having pulled up in both runs following a long absence

races.race_name: Racey Stranger's Birthday Bash Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Rapid Mission
runners.spotlight: Flat winner who is 0-10 over hurdles and has yet to threaten in his handicaps from 2m1f to 3m; tried cheekpieces last time and now goes in a visor; risky.
runners.comment: 0-10 over hurdles and has yet to threaten in his handicaps from 2m1f to 3m

runners.name: Answer That
runners.spotlight: Had form in Irish bumpers; steadily progressive profile over hurdles, last time going down by 8l on handicap debut at Plumpton (2m, good); lacks a change of gear so going up in trip could help matters, as might the first-time hood.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive; lacks a change of gear so going up in trip could help matters

runners.name: Mystical Mabel
runners.spotlight: Well held on the Flat and already looking limited over hurdles; behind Answer That last time.
runners.comment: Well held on the Flat and looking limited over hurdles; behind Answer That last time

runners.name: No Mean Feat
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden and has been quiet in four runs after a long absence; no obvious significance in the return of cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden and has been quiet in four runs after a long absence

runners.name: Man Oh Man
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent maiden but only his second run in this headgear combination when going close at Exeter (2m7f, good); with competitive form over this shorter trip, he's one of the better options.
runners.comment: Only his second run in this headgear combination when going close at Exeter

runners.name: Anthracite
runners.spotlight: His first effort of note when beaten just over 5l in a Wincanton handicap (1m7f, good; first-time cheekpieces) in which he rallied gamely after stumbling three out; now he's of interest.
runners.comment: His first effort of note when beaten just over 5l at Wincanton; that can be upgraded

runners.name: Jacquetta
runners.spotlight: Backed into 7-2 for his handicap debut here last month when also tried in cheekpieces, but weakened when it mattered to be about 10l off the principals; could still build on that as it's early days.
runners.comment: Beaten about 10l on handicap debut; could still build on that as it's early days

runners.name: Scoresby
runners.spotlight: Won his sixth point in March but was pulled up in his two previous races over hurdles (maiden/novice) and wouldn't be the percentage call here in his first handicap.
runners.comment: Won his sixth point in March but was pulled up in his two previous races over hurdles

runners.name: Callero
runners.spotlight: Poor form on the Flat and in his qualifying races over hurdles; partnered by a 10lb claimer for this handicap debut and couldn't be confident.
runners.comment: Poor form on the Flat and in his qualifying races over hurdles; handicap debut

races.race_name: Summerley Recruitment Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Best Of British Sporting Lunches Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Major Dino
runners.spotlight: One of the favourites when well down the field on debut at Kempton (good; raced too freely) last year and showed more in his belated second race when third at Hereford (good); that form sets the standard.
runners.comment: Third at Hereford after a year out and that form sets the standard

runners.name: Tommy Tipa
runners.spotlight: First foal out of an 11.6f Flat and 2m/2m1f hurdle winner; trainer had a debutant win a bumper in September; immediate fitting of a hood is a tad unnerving.
runners.comment: Newcomer for whom the immediate fitting of a hood is a tad unnerving

runners.name: Blackmore Jack
runners.spotlight: Hood added after finishing completely tailed off on good ground at Wincanton (150-1).
runners.comment: Hood added after finishing completely tailed off on good ground at Wincanton (150-1)
runners.owner: The Cant Take It With You Syndicate

runners.name: Knight Crossing
runners.spotlight: For one bred to stay much further she made an encouraging start when beaten 14l at Uttoxeter (soft), behind horses from top yards; should go well.
runners.comment: Bred to stay much further so made an encouraging start when beaten 14l at Uttoxeter

runners.name: Lightsoutandaway
runners.spotlight: Brother to three Flat winners and half-brother to six further ones, including Legionar (bumper and 2m3f-2m5f hurdle); the betting could be informative on debut.
runners.comment: Related to winners under both codes; the betting could be informative on debut

runners.name: Savanna Storm
runners.spotlight: 12,000euros 3yo; first foal; dam unplaced in bumpers/2m2f-3m hurdles, half-sister to smart 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Off You Go, out of unraced close relative of Irish Grand National winner Shutthefrontdoor; the market should guide as to expectations.
runners.comment: 12,000euros 3yo; first foal; dam unplaced in bumpers/2m2f-3m hurdle

runners.name: The Drunken Sailor
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam unplaced in bumper and 2m3f-3m1f hurdles, half-sister to bumper and useful 2m1f-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Watcombe Heights; trainer does well in bumpers and he could go well on debut.
runners.comment: Trainer does well in bumpers and he could go well on debut

runners.name: Magnifix Du Moulin
runners.spotlight: Close third in a 1m4f French bumper on soft ground in October when tongue tied; interesting to see what the market makes of her.
runners.comment: Close third in a 1m4f French bumper on soft ground in October when tongue tied

runners.name: Maucourt d'Airy
runners.spotlight: A tailed-off last of the four runners on heavy ground at Ffos Las two months ago (15-2); now has tongue tied.
runners.comment: A tailed-off last of the four runners on heavy ground at Ffos Las two months ago (15-2)

races.race_name: Osmanthus Handicap (Class 5) (All-Weather Track) (Dirt)

runners.name: Mega Force
runners.spotlight: 1-7 for Aidan O'Brien in Ireland, but unplaced in all 17 local starts on turf/dirt and no improvement for the drop to this grade last time; blinkers return.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all 17 local starts; no improvement for the drop to this grade last time

runners.name: Oriental Surprise
runners.spotlight: Has gone close a few times over 5f/6f on turf at Happy Valley this season, but now 0-24; plenty to prove back up in trip returned to dirt and he has a poor draw.
runners.comment: 0-24; plenty to prove back up in trip returned to dirt and he has a poor draw

runners.name: Sparkle And Gold
runners.spotlight: Reserve; unplaced in all 13 starts and still has stamina to prove; unraced on dirt.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all 13 starts and still has stamina to prove; unraced on dirt

runners.name: Floof
runners.spotlight: 1-22 in Hong Kong, the win coming over C&D in September 2024; below form lately, though, and the drop to Class 5 made little difference last time; opposable from his high draw.
runners.comment: C&D winner, but below form lately and has a high draw

runners.name: Fortune Warrior
runners.spotlight: Just 2-40 locally and has barely beaten a rival in his last seven starts; yet to race beyond 6f in Hong Kong, though he did win over a similar trip to this in Australia; cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: 2-40 locally and has barely beaten a rival in his last seven starts; cheekpieces return

runners.name: Meepmeep
runners.spotlight: Just 1-31, though the win came over C&D around this time last year and he finished runner-up to both Go Go Go and Meepmeep back here this spring; well drawn and a player again.
runners.comment: 1-31, though the win came over C&D and he twice finished runner-up this spring; player

runners.name: Phantom Cyclone
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over C&D in January, but not matched that form in two starts since and now 0-22 in Hong Kong; others stronger.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D in January, but not matched that form since and now 0-22 in Hong Kong

runners.name: All Eyes On Me
runners.spotlight: Twice made the frame over C&D earlier in year, but now 0-18, has the outside stall and is 5lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Twice made the frame over C&D earlier in year, but now 0-18 and has the outside stall
runners.owner: Enrico Chow Yue Hin & Jennifer Ng Mau Yee

runners.name: Go Go Go
runners.spotlight: 2-39, his latest win when when making all to beat Meepmeep over C&D in March; finished behind a couple of these back here five weeks ago, but he had a wide draw then; could go well.
runners.comment: Behind a couple of these over C&D five weeks ago, but had a wide draw then; could go well

runners.name: Happydearhappydeer
runners.spotlight: Placed in three consecutive starts on dirt (1m/1m1f) the winter before last, but hasn't shown much in four outings since returning from a year off in January and is now 0-13; others preferred.
runners.comment: Not shown much since returning from a year off in January and now 0-13; others preferred
runners.owner: Hong Yiu, Lynn Fu Lam Wu Et Al

runners.name: Lucky Generations
runners.spotlight: 2-22 with both wins coming over 6f on turf at Happy Valley this season; still 6lb higher than for the latest, while the step back up in trip, return to dirt and high draw also leave him with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: The step back up in trip, return to dirt and high draw all leave him with a bit to prove

runners.name: Concorde Star
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 16 starts came on turf at Happy Valley (6f) in December; ran poorly in one go on dirt (6f) and has never attempted this far before; needs the new eyeshield to make a difference.
runners.comment: Ran poorly in one go on dirt and has never attempted this far before; eyeshield on
runners.owner: Philip Yu Chi Wai & Dr Jennie Peterson Yu

runners.name: Hailtothevictors
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win came over C&D early last month (beating Meepmeep by a head) and he was only beaten around half a length into fourth over a furlong further 18 days later; high on list.
runners.comment: Won over C&D early last month and a close fourth over 1m1f last time; high on list
runners.owner: Graham Kot Li Heng & Amy Chau

runners.name: Smart City
runners.spotlight: 1-27 in Hong Kong; drop in class is a postive as is Zac Purton regaining the ride, but he ran poorly in one previous attempt on dirt (6f) which leaves him with something to prove.
runners.comment: 1-27 in Hong Kong; drops in class but he ran poorly in one previous attempt on dirt

runners.name: Spangle Fortune
runners.spotlight: 2-36 in Hong Kong, though has plenty of form on dirt here including both wins (6f/1m); needs to put his last three performances well behind him, though.
runners.comment: Dual winner on dirt here, but needs to put his last three performances well behind him

runners.name: Super Hong Kong
runners.spotlight: Reserve; six-time winner over further on turf; has run well on dirt, but not in his latest outing; needs to bounce back if getting a run.
runners.comment: Six-time winner over further on turf; has run well on dirt, but not in his latest outing

races.race_name: Magnolia Handicap (Class 5) (All-Weather Track) (Dirt)

runners.name: Bingo Babe
runners.spotlight: 0-20 and although not beaten so far in his last three starts, is still opposable from 3lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: 0-20 and opposable from 3lb out of the weights
runners.owner: Charmaine Wong Kam Mei & Chan Fu Ho

runners.name: Taihang Scenery
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 0-17, but has made the frame a few times including over C&D; creditable efforts from wide draws on turf in his last two starts and frame material again if getting a run.
runners.comment: 0-17, but has made the frame a few times including over C&D; frame material again

runners.name: Brilliant Fire
runners.spotlight: Well drawn, but out of the frame in all 14 starts despite a sliding mark; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all 14 starts despite a sliding mark; hard to make a case for

runners.name: Spicy Spangle
runners.spotlight: Handy sort who is 2-28 with both wins coming on turf (5f/6f); well held in three goes over C&D, though, so has something to prove.
runners.comment: Well held in three goes over C&D so has something to prove

runners.name: Robot Knight
runners.spotlight: 1-26, albeit the win came over C&D and he has been placed on several other occasions under these conditions; has a favourable draw and could go well.
runners.comment: 1-26, though the win came over C&D and he has been placed on several other occasions here

runners.name: Run Yes Run
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed a couple of times early in his career, but not built on it and now 0-15; will make little appeal on dirt debut if getting a run.
runners.comment: Placed a couple of times early in his career, but not built on it and now 0-15; dirt debut
runners.owner: Kong Wing Ming & Edmond Kong Chun Man

runners.name: Macanese Master
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose record since December reads 131222 including finishing second and third in two starts over C&D; could take some catching; high on list.
runners.comment: Record since December reads 131222 including finishing second and third over C&D

runners.name: Viva Chaleur
runners.spotlight: Running well over C&D recently, winning comfortably early last month and running Noble Deluxe to half a length 17 days ago; enjoys a 5lb pull and is better drawn than his old rival; respected.
runners.comment: C&D winner and weighted to reverse latest course running with Noble Deluxe; respected

runners.name: Noble Deluxe
runners.spotlight: Record over C&D this year reads 131, beating Viva Chaleur by half a length 17 days ago; clearly enjoys the conditions, but a 6lb rise and his wide draw will make things a bit more tricky.
runners.comment: Record over C&D this year reads 131; 6lb rise and wide draw make things a bit more tricky

runners.name: Happy Action
runners.spotlight: 0-9, but finished runner-up in a Class 4 over C&D in October and now drops in grade; has the plum draw and one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: 0-9, but drops in grade and has the plum draw; one for the shortlist

runners.name: No Other Choice
runners.spotlight: 0-12 and has a high draw, but he has twice been placed from even wider stalls over C&D this season; far from impossible he could make an impact.
runners.comment: 0-12; drawn wide but has twice been placed from high draws over C&D this season
runners.owner: Cheng Chung Wah & Enzo Cheng Tin Hang

runners.name: Shinyu Kokoroe
runners.spotlight: 2-2 on synthetics in Australia, but out of the frame in all 12 starts in Hong Kong, eight of them on dirt; another drop in grade the one positive.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all 12 starts in Hong Kong; another drop in grade the one positive

runners.name: Top To Sky
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in nine starts on turf; improvement needed switched to dirt with blinkers added and the outside stall isn't much help.
runners.comment: 0-9; improvement needed switched to dirt and drawn widest; blinkers on

runners.name: Von Baer
runners.spotlight: 2-7 in Britain, but 0-19 in Hong Kong; needs the change of stable and application of a tongue-tie to have an effect.
runners.comment: 0-19 in Hong Kong; needs change of stable and application of tongue-tie to have an effect

races.race_name: Carnation Handicap (Class 4) (Dirt) (Div I)

runners.name: Lakeshore Hero
runners.spotlight: 1lb lower than for his successful reappearance over 6f on turf at Happy Valley in November, but not built on it and has been well held in both goes on dirt (1m); look elsewhere.
runners.comment: 1lb lower than for his successful reappearance, but well held in both goes on dirt

runners.name: Zeta Hedge
runners.spotlight: Finished runner-up on his debut over 5f on turf at Happy Valley in October, but has gone badly the wrong way since; needs the switch to dirt to help stop the rot.
runners.comment: Runner-up on Happy Valley debut in October, but gone badly the wrong way since; dirt debut

runners.name: Charming Babe
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual turf winner at Happy Valley (6f) and 2lb lower than for the most recent; may have found 7f stretching him when returned to this grade last time, but one previous go on dirt proved inconclusive.
runners.comment: 2lb below last winning mark, but one previous go on dirt proved inconclusive

runners.name: Juicy Dragon
runners.spotlight: Seems happier on dirt (354125) than turf (08005) and better drawn than for his last two starts; should be bang there.
runners.comment: Seems happier on dirt (354125) than turf (08005); should be bang there

runners.name: Vulcanus
runners.spotlight: Three of his four wins have come on this surface and he drops back into a Class 4 for the first time since winning over C&D in January; could have been better drawn, but still a player.
runners.comment: Drops back into a Class 4 for the first time since winning over C&D in January; a player

runners.name: Joy Star
runners.spotlight: Limited encouragement in four starts including one over C&D; cheekpieces may help, but he has a wide draw to overcome.
runners.comment: Limited encouragement in four starts; has a wide draw to overcome; cheekpieces on
runners.owner: Lam King Wai & Dr & Mrs Lam Kai Cheong

runners.name: Cloud Nine
runners.spotlight: Handy sort who has shown ability in three of his four starts on turf, but the switch to the dirt and the outside stall pose questions; blinkers are given a go.
runners.comment: Has shown ability on turf, but dirt debut and outside stall pose questions; blinkers on

runners.name: Topspin King
runners.spotlight: Hasn't beaten many in three starts on turf at Happy Valley (5f/6f); needs to improve for the switch to dirt, though he does have the plum inside stall.
runners.comment: Hasn't beaten many in three starts on turf; needs to improve for the switch to dirt
runners.owner: John Hui Kin Yip & William Hui Chun Lim

runners.name: Kol
runners.spotlight: Some encouraging trials, but beat only one home on his official debut over C&D five weeks ago; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Only beat one home on his official debut over C&D five weeks ago; best watched for now

runners.name: Mega Captain
runners.spotlight: Plenty of ability in his first three trials (121), but well held on his official debut here (5f, turf) last month; has since won another trial (over C&D) so he is well worth keeping an eye on; tongue-tie given a go.
runners.comment: Well held on his official debut, but record in trials reads 1211; tongue-tie on
runners.owner: Leung Tin Fu, Leung Kit Faat & Ma Hing Man

runners.name: Perfect Triumph
runners.spotlight: Fourth over 5f on turf here in March was much better than his efforts either side; needs more now switched to dirt with an eyeshield fitted.
runners.comment: Ability in one of his three starts on turf, but needs more switched to dirt; eyeshield on

runners.name: Prestige Win
runners.spotlight: Drops in grade after three moderate efforts on turf since arriving in Hong Kong; placed in a dirt trial here at the start of the year; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Drops in grade after three moderate efforts in Hong Kong; placed in a dirt trial here

runners.name: Bullish Nova
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has shown promise in his last two trials over this C&D, but will need a good deal more if he is to make an official winning debut.
runners.comment: Has shown promise in his last two trials, but will need a good deal more on official debut

runners.name: Sight Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over 1m on this surface durng the winter, but proved a disappointing favourite in February and has since finished well held in a barrier trial over C&D; has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Dual winner over 1m on dirt during the winter, but was a disappointing favourite last time

races.race_name: Lotus Handicap (Class 4) (All-Weather Track) (Dirt)

runners.name: Ariel
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner; three wins on dirt including one over this trip, but has regressed dramatically since the autumn despite a big slide down the weights; best watched for now.
runners.comment: C&D winner, but has regressed dramatically since the autumn; best watched for now

runners.name: The Lion King
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in Australia, but 0-12 in Hong Kong; finished behind three of these in returning blinkers over C&D early last month and will need to do better.
runners.comment: 0-12 in Hong Kong; finished behind three of these over C&D early last month

runners.name: Firefoot
runners.spotlight: 1-28, but mainly running well on dirt this year and was only beaten a short head over C&D 17 days ago; up 1lb but again shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Only beaten a short head over C&D 17 days ago; up 1lb but again shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Enthralled
runners.spotlight: 1-18 in Hong Kong, but continues to slide down the weights and is 7lb below his winning mark; unraced on dirt, but is a half-brother to a three-time AW winner; interesting with Zac Purton keeping the ride.
runners.comment: 1-18 in Hong Kong; 7lb below his last winning mark; half-brother to a three-time AW winner

runners.name: Glorious Success
runners.spotlight: 1-9 in Australia and 0-16 in Hong Kong; finished behind four of these over C&D five weeks ago; blinkers return, but much more still needed.
runners.comment: 0-16 in Hong Kong; finished behind four of these over C&D five weeks ago; blinkers return

runners.name: Glorious St Pauls
runners.spotlight: Safely held in first four starts and finished behind two of these on his dirt debut (1m) 17 days ago; improvement needed.
runners.comment: 0-4 and finished behind two of these on his dirt debut 17 days ago; improvement needed

runners.name: Yoda's Choice
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come on dirt at Class 5 level, but he was only beaten a short head by Happy Universe in a Class 4 over C&D last time and is now 3lb better off, plus his rider's 10lb claim; high on list.
runners.comment: Both wins at Class 5 level, but is weighted to reverse latest C&D form with Happy Universe
runners.owner: Alan Lee Yu Sing & Exors Of The Estate Of The Late Lee Kwok

runners.name: Viva Taste
runners.spotlight: 0-11; finished runner-up on her reappearance over C&D in October, but hasn't fared any better since and is 2lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: 0-11; runner-up over C&D in October, but hasn't fared any better since; 2lb wrong
runners.owner: Jimmy Chung Kwok Man & Dominic Chung Won Lok

runners.name: Happy Universe
runners.spotlight: Battled on well when beating Yoda's Choice by a short head over C&D five weeks ago, but may struggle to confirm the placings on 3lb worse terms.
runners.comment: Beat Yoda's Choice by a short head over C&D five weeks ago, but is now 3lb worse off

runners.name: Perfect Team
runners.spotlight: In the doldrums for a while, but a drop in grade resulted in a much better effort when a close fifth over 1m on dirt here 17 days ago; very well treated on the form he was showing early last year; interesting.
runners.comment: Better effort last time and very well treated on the form he was showing early last year
runners.owner: Ringo Ng Yiu Kwok & Lisa Ng Chan Choi Wan

runners.name: Forever Folks
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent; three-time winner on dirt, the latest over this trip in January; the rise in the weights seems to have found him out in both outings since, though.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in January, but a rise in the weights seems to have found him out twice since

races.race_name: Carnation Handicap (Class 4) (All-Weather Track) (Dirt) (Div II)

runners.name: California Bay
runners.spotlight: 0-18 including two starts in Australia; 3lb lower than when beaten a short head on turf here (7f) in March, but not built on it and dirt is an unknown.
runners.comment: 0-18; beaten a short head on turf here in March, but not built on it; dirt is an unknown

runners.name: Light Years Glory
runners.spotlight: 0-9 since his successful debut on turf here (6f) just under a year ago, but has run some good races as when third over C&D 17 days ago; competes off the same mark and should go well.
runners.comment: Third over C&D 17 days ago; races off the same mark and should go well

runners.name: One Man Show
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in Australia but 0-9 in Hong Kong; twice placed over C&D at the start of the year, but has regressed since and he has an awkward draw; has worn cheekpieces and a tongue-tie before, but not together.
runners.comment: Twice placed over C&D at the start of the year, but has regressed since; drawn wide
runners.owner: 24/25 Frankie Lor Fu Chuen Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Jolly Companion
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual C&D winner towards the end of 2024 and scored on turf at Happy Valley (6f) in January; still 5lb higher and may need to drop a bit more in the weights.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; still 5lb higher than when successful at Happy Valley in January

runners.name: Regrowth Winner
runners.spotlight: Showed little in five starts, the latest over C&D in first-time blinkers; opposable from his wide draw and should be eligible for a drop in grade soon.
runners.comment: Showed little in five starts, the latest over C&D; opposable from his wide draw
runners.owner: Chan Wing Kwan, Chan Wai Leung & Chadwick Mok Cham Hung

runners.name: Tourbillon Golfer
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner; not at his best on turf here (5f) last time, but had previously finished a good third over C&D when making his dirt debut; again has the plum draw from which to attack; major player.
runners.comment: Ran well when third over C&D on penultimate start; has the plum draw from which to attack
runners.owner: Shum Mak Ling, Justin Liu Sai Wai & Wilson Liu Chung Kin

runners.name: Flying Sniper
runners.spotlight: Midfield finishes in three starts over 6f on turf, but not beaten far in his last two outings and was drawn wide last time; still needs a bit more now switched to dirt.
runners.comment: Midfield finishes in three starts over 6f on turf, needs a bit more now switched to dirt
runners.owner: Raymond Tsang Heung Tak & Kenneth Tsang Heung Kan

runners.name: Magic Super
runners.spotlight: Hasn't beaten many in five starts on turf (5f-7f) for another yard; needs a transformation now switched to dirt with an eyeshield applied.
runners.comment: Not beaten many in five starts; needs a transformation on stable/dirt debut; eyeshield on

runners.name: Supreme Voyager
runners.spotlight: Mixed form in five starts on turf here, his head defeat from the outside stall over 6f in March standing out; each-way claims if repeating that performance on dirt debut.
runners.comment: Beaten a head on turf here in March; each-way claims if repeating that performance on dirt
runners.owner: 24/25 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Casa Primo
runners.spotlight: Reserve; fifth in two starts over 5f on turf here; more needed up a furlong on dirt debut.
runners.comment: Fifth in two starts over 5f on turf here; more needed up a furlong on dirt debut

runners.name: Diamond Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Midfield on his official debut on turf at Happy Valley (6f) in March, but finished runner-up in barrier trials over this C&D either side; could play a part if tapping into that.
runners.comment: Midfield on debut at Happy Valley, but second in barrier trials over this C&D either side
runners.owner: Lau So Wah & Donna Ng Siu Ying

runners.name: Forever Fancy
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in three starts over 6f/7f on turf late last year; finished last in a turf trial last month, but had previously won a dirt trial; not quite sure what to expect.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in three starts on turf late last year; has since won a dirt trial
runners.owner: James Lau Chi Wing, Lau Kai Tai & Michelle Lau Wing Sheung

runners.name: Mega Awesome
runners.spotlight: Has shown some ability in his trials and is worth a second look with Zac Purton taking the ride on official debut.
runners.comment: Has shown some ability in his trials; worth a second look with Zac Purton taking the ride
runners.owner: Joyce Lee & Alan Kiang Ping Fai

runners.name: Word Of Kindness
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 6f on turf late last year, his fourth at Happy Valley a big improvement from his debut here; will need to improve on his subsequent trials, though.
runners.comment: Fourth on turf at Happy Valley in December, but needs to improve on his subsequent trials

races.race_name: Chamomile Handicap (Class 4) (All-Weather Track) (Dirt)

runners.name: Leaping Star
runners.spotlight: Quite useful when based in Ireland but has finished well down the field on all nine Hong Kong starts; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Quite useful when based in Ireland but well down the field on all nine HK starts
runners.owner: Maurice Lai Tai Hei, John Lo Et Al

runners.name: Daily Trophy
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly when midfield over C&D on last two appearances, but sprinting is more his game and he's drawn wide.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly when midfield over C&D on last two starts but sprinting is more his game
runners.owner: Peter Cheng & Jerry Cheng Chun Ho

runners.name: Fashion Legend
runners.spotlight: Rather inconsistent this season but was denied a clear run on both C&D starts last month and is now 7lb lower than when second over C&D in December; can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Rather inconsistent this season but now 7lb lower than when second over C&D in December
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui

runners.name: Night Purosangue
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over C&D for new stable this season, but didn't fire in race won by Blossomy here last month and his run of poor luck on the draw front has continued.
runners.comment: Didn't fire here last month and his run of poor luck on the draw front has continued

runners.name: Bull Attitude
runners.spotlight: Has modest 1-18 strike-rate but has made the frame four consecutive times over C&D since switched to dirt in February, most recently when second to Blossomy 17 days ago; well drawn today; ought to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: In the frame four consecutive times over C&D since switched to dirt in February; a player

runners.name: Shotgun
runners.spotlight: Made all twice on turf this season (6f/7f); simultaneously proved his stamina and ability to act on dirt when close third over C&D last month but wide draw makes this a tough task.
runners.comment: Front-runner; ran well over C&D when upped in trip last month but has tough draw today

runners.name: Hinokami Kagura
runners.spotlight: Out of form over 6f this year but belied 50-1 odds and was close third over C&D when upped in trip last month; it's hard to tell whether he'll be in the right mood again but he could plausibly have more to offer over 1m.
runners.comment: Belied 50-1 odds when close third over C&D last month, his first attempt at 1m

runners.name: Never Peter Out
runners.spotlight: Seemingly on the downgrade now, but his last three runs (all over C&D) were perfectly respectable and Zac Purton is booked for the second start running; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: On the downgrade now but his last three runs (all over C&D) were perfectly respectable

runners.name: Blossomy
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who displayed improvement to open his account over C&D last month; 6lb higher now but that was just his second dirt start and he may still have potential.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo who displayed improvement to open his account over C&D last month

runners.name: Chill Kaka
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner last season, but placed only once from nine starts this term and his last couple of efforts have been very disappointing.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner last season but mostly out of form this term

runners.name: Allcash
runners.spotlight: Won on turf in November and was subsequently placed twice over C&D in higher-grade company, but has suffered a slump in form in recent months and is drawn on the outside today.
runners.comment: Placed twice in higher-grade company this season but has suffered a slump in form

runners.name: California Star
runners.spotlight: Won readily over C&D in February and went close here the following month; good claims if judged on those efforts but has dropped his guard lately.
runners.comment: Began year in good form over C&D but has dropped his guard lately

runners.name: Luck Is Back
runners.spotlight: 0-16 in Hong Kong and was inconsistent for Francis Lui in first half of this season, but makes stable debut from a workable mark and this C&D suits; could have a say if fully tuned up after 115-day break.
runners.comment: Inconsistent this season but makes stable debut from workable mark and this C&D suits

races.race_name: Carnation Handicap (Class 4) (All-Weather Track) (Dirt) (Div III)

runners.name: Bigtime Generation
runners.spotlight: Placed only once from nine turf starts in Hong Kong; well drawn for this dirt debut but needs the new surface to show him in a better light.
runners.comment: Placed only once from nine turf starts in Hong Kong; makes dirt debut today

runners.name: Good Luck Babe
runners.spotlight: Now 5lb lower than when winning on turf (7f) in October, but has not been firing on all cylinders in recent months and has a tough draw.
runners.comment: Not firing on all cylinders in recent months and has a tough draw

runners.name: Silver Spurs
runners.spotlight: Needs to better his recent turf efforts, but is now 5lb lower than when placed over 5f in February and should be okay back over this C&D; Joao Moreira keeps the ride; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Needs to better recent turf form but is on a good mark and should be okay back on dirt

runners.name: Fun N Fun Together
runners.spotlight: Seemingly back in good nick when close third over C&D last month and remains 1lb lower than for C&D win under Zac Purton September; reunited with the champion jockey today; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in good nick over C&D last month; reunited with Zac Purton today

runners.name: Conrad The Great
runners.spotlight: Disappointed here last month and is now 0-8, but was runner-up over C&D on his two previous outings and has obvious each-way claims if judged on those efforts.
runners.comment: Disappointed last time but was second over C&D twice beforehand
runners.owner: Tam Man Wai, Wilson Tam Man Shu & Dr Tam Po Chor

runners.name: Full Of Laughter
runners.spotlight: Made quite promising debut over 5f on turf here in December, but subsequent form has been very disappointing and he's drawn wide for this dirt debut; needs a huge boost from the new blinkers.
runners.comment: Made quite promising debut in December but subsequent turf form has been disappointing

runners.name: Mapogo
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who displayed clear promise over 5f on first two turf starts and was quite impressive in a C&D trial in between; makes official dirt debut today; dangerous to ignore.
runners.comment: Displayed clear promise on both turf starts and impressed in C&D trial in between
runners.owner: Daniel Chong Kwok Ming, Henry Chan Lai Yue & Ng Yim Ping

runners.name: Sky Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Took big step in right direction when fifth on turf here in February, his third start, but has wide draw for dirt debut and further progress is required.
runners.comment: Latest turf run was more encouraging but he's draw wide for dirt debut

runners.name: Invictus Dragon
runners.spotlight: Reserve; ten-race maiden; placed twice on turf in March (6f/7f) and didn't run badly when fifth here last month; will be making his dirt debut if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden; placed twice on turf in March (6f/7f); dirt debut today

runners.name: Starry Show
runners.spotlight: Reserve; not disgraced when midfield on turf at Happy Valley a week ago and continues to slip down the weights, but is yet to be placed after nine Hong Kong starts (one on dirt).
runners.comment: Yet to be placed after nine Hong Kong starts (one on dirt)

runners.name: Good Chap
runners.spotlight: 0-6 on turf but began dirt career with two C&D wins in February/March; managed only seventh here last month but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Began dirt career with two C&D wins; held since but still enters calculations
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Edmund Hon Hing Cheung

runners.name: Notthesillyone
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D towards end of last year and was also on target over 5f on turf in March; may have had too much use made of him when only ninth over C&D last month and remains of interest.
runners.comment: Three wins (two over C&D) this season; went off too hard last time; can rebound

runners.name: Natural High
runners.spotlight: Came good for Dennis Yip at third attempt with narrow C&D success in first-time visor last month; probably still on a workable mark after recent 6lb rise and has the inside draw; respected.
runners.comment: Still on workable mark after last month's C&D win and has good draw; respected

runners.name: Happy Shooter
runners.spotlight: Below form when last in action (raced too freely) but had been in good nick on turf beforehand; can feature from favourable draw if suited by this switch to dirt.
runners.comment: Below form last time (raced too freely) but had been in good nick on turf beforehand
runners.owner: 23/24 Danny Shum Chap Shing Trainer Syndicate

races.race_name: Camellia Handicap (Class 3) (All-Weather Track) (Dirt)

runners.name: Glittering Legend
runners.spotlight: Dual Polytrack winner in Britain; took big step forward when fourth on turf here (1m) in March, his third Hong Kong start, but his latest run was disappointing and he's not easy to predict on this dirt debut.
runners.comment: Took big step forward when fourth here in March but well held since; hard to predict

runners.name: Noisy Boy
runners.spotlight: Emerged from spell in the doldrums with close sixth in C&D contest won by Loch Tay last month and his subsequent turf run was respectable, but he's poorly drawn today and others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Close sixth over C&D last month and has since run okay on turf but others appeal more
runners.owner: Leung Lun Ping & Ricky Leung Ghin Ming

runners.name: Nezha
runners.spotlight: Came good in Hong Kong with C&D win on dirt debut in March, his seventh attempt; not beaten far when seventh in race won by Loch Tay last month and could still have a bigger run in him on this surface.
runners.comment: C&D winner on dirt debut in March; held since but may have more to offer on this surface

runners.name: Fortunate Son
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner in Class 4 this season, but has found things a bit tougher since upped to this grade and needs this switch to dirt to enable further progress.
runners.comment: Has found things quite tough since upped to this grade; makes dirt debut today

runners.name: Supreme Agility
runners.spotlight: Gained last-gasp C&D win in November and has made the frame on both subsequent dirt starts; evidently in good nick but combination of wide draw and rise in grade demands more from him.
runners.comment: In good form on dirt this season but up in grade today and has outside draw
runners.owner: Rotary Club of Sha Tin Syndicate

runners.name: Endeared
runners.spotlight: Very close third behind Loch Tay and Talents Ambition over C&D last month and also ran well on his two 7f turf starts either side of that; holds no secrets from the handicapper but has another big role to play from stall 2.
runners.comment: Very close third over C&D last month and also ran well on both turf starts either side

runners.name: Turin Mascot
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last season; well below best for Pierre Ng this term but makes stable debut from attractive mark and has inside draw; could be dangerous if combination of 81-day break and change of regime has done him good.
runners.comment: Out of form this season but has attractive mark and favourable draw for stable debut
runners.owner: Dr Sheldon Tse Hiu Tung, Ken Huang Jian Hua & Tsoi Huk Yam

runners.name: Master Trillion
runners.spotlight: Reserve; first past the post (subsequently demoted to second) in Class 4 turf contest in March, but well beaten in this higher grade twice since; will be making dirt debut if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Well beaten on last two turf starts; makes dirt debut today

runners.name: Sky Vino
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace for C&D win on February and returns to dirt after going very close on turf at Happy Valley last month; nudged up 2lb to another career-high mark but still considered.
runners.comment: C&D winner in February and returns here after going close on turf last month

runners.name: Sword Point
runners.spotlight: Generally very disappointing this season but stopped the rot with respectable sixth on turf (1m1f) off much-reduced mark last month; will be fine back on dirt; interesting to see if he can build on that recent effort.
runners.comment: Generally very disappointing this season but latest run stopped the rot

runners.name: Dragon Air Force
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in January and ran at least as well when close second here the following month; not at same level on last two outings but, if Nichola Yuen's useful 10lb claim is considered, he's still on a good mark.
runners.comment: Not at best lately but began this year in good form over C&D; might bounce back
runners.owner: Au Kai Kwong & Michelle Jessica Au

runners.name: Loch Tay
runners.spotlight: Just held on to pip Talents Ambition over C&D last month and is now 2-3 on dirt; might have more to offer on this surface but has 6lb rise and wide draw to contend with today.
runners.comment: 2-3 on dirt after last month's narrow C&D win; 6lb higher and drawn wide today

runners.name: Steps Ahead
runners.spotlight: Consistent over 7f/1m on turf in early part of this season, but returned from break with two below-par efforts in March/April and has a bit to prove on this dirt debut.
runners.comment: Below par on both turf starts since a break; has a bit to prove on dirt debut
runners.owner: Yeung Sing Tung, Sunny Yeung Wah Hon & Janice Yeung Wah Kei

runners.name: Lucky My Way
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual 6f turf winner last season but out of form over 7f this term; upped in trip again and switched to dirt for stable debut.
runners.comment: Dual 6f turf winner last season but out of form over 7f this term; stable/dirt debut
runners.owner: Patrick Wong Ho Yin, Ricky Choy Wing Kay Et Al

runners.name: Talents Ambition
runners.spotlight: Gained all three Hong Kong wins over C&D and lost out only by a nose to Loch Tay over C&D on latest outing five weeks ago; usually ridden patiently so his wide draw is not a huge inconvenience; likely contender.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; went close here last month; likely contender again

runners.name: Winning Dragon
runners.spotlight: Not seen on dirt since finishing well down the field over C&D in January 2023 and not seen at all since underwhelming seasonal debut on turf in October; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Absent since underwhelming seasonal debut in October and not yet proven on dirt
runners.owner: Alexander Yeung Kwok Kai, Edward Yeung Et Al

races.race_name: Begonia Handicap (Class 3) (All-Weather Track) (Dirt)

runners.name: Victor The Winner
runners.spotlight: Reserve; Group 1 turf winner in his prime but badly out of form this season, albeit not beaten far when seventh last time; has left it late in the day to launch his dirt career.
runners.comment: Group 1 turf winner in his prime but badly out of form this season

runners.name: Must Go
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to exploit much-reduced mark last month, recording a sixth C&D success; recent 7lb rise might leave him vulnerable today, though.
runners.comment: Exploited much-reduced mark over C&D last month but might be vulnerable after 7lb rise

runners.name: Super Joy N Fun
runners.spotlight: Emerged from spell in the doldrums with keeping-on fourth on turf last month, but is still 3lb higher than when winning over C&D in October; others have more persuasive profiles.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in form on turf last month but others have more persuasive profiles
runners.owner: Johnny Wong Chun Nam, Nancy Wong Et Al

runners.name: Target Audience
runners.spotlight: Made all over C&D on last month's dirt debut; back up in grade today but still unexposed on this surface and has another favourable draw; no surprise if he gives this a good shot.
runners.comment: Up in grade after last month's C&D win but still unexposed on dirt and has good draw

runners.name: Mr Energia
runners.spotlight: Reserve; well suited by dirt but has lost his way a bit since C&D win in October and is still on a fairly tough mark.
runners.comment: Has lost his way a bit since C&D win in October and is still on a fairly tough mark

runners.name: City Gold Banner
runners.spotlight: Impressed when winning readily here (5f, turf) on Hong Kong debut in January, but two lesser turf runs have followed (5f/6f); deserted by Zac Purton today and has a point to prove on dirt debut.
runners.comment: Impressed on HK debut but two lesser turf runs have followed; now makes dirt debut

runners.name: Blazing Wind
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season; made the frame over C&D on last two appearances, despite not enjoying the run of the race on either occasion, and is reunited with Zac Purton today; must be considered.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season and still in prime form; must be considered

runners.name: Giant Spirit
runners.spotlight: Well down the field at triple-digit odds on all three Hong Kong starts (5f-6f, turf) and has outside draw for dirt debut.
runners.comment: Well down the field at triple-digit odds on all three Hong Kong starts
runners.owner: Dr V Chan Chi Tao, John Tang Lam Chek & Uri Yau Wing Hay

runners.name: Aurora Patch
runners.spotlight: Dirt and dual turf winner this season; runner-up over C&D on consecutive outings last month and might still be improving, but double-digit draw a negative today.
runners.comment: Second twice over C&D last month but double-digit draw a negative today

runners.name: High Praise
runners.spotlight: 1-5 in Australia, the win gained in a 1m maiden in March 2025; makes Hong Kong/dirt debut after eight-month break and is probably best watched.
runners.comment: 1-5 in Australia, the win gained in a 1m maiden in March 2025; makes Hong Kong/dirt debut
runners.owner: Anthony Poon Tak Ching, Vivica Poon Ip Sze Man et al

runners.name: Galactic Voyage
runners.spotlight: 2-6 on turf during this first season in Hong Kong and yet to run a bad race; can make his presence felt again if suited by switch to dirt.
runners.comment: 2-6 on turf during this first season in Hong Kong and yet to run a bad race; dirt debut
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Lam Ngai And Emily Lam Huen

runners.name: Symbol Of Strength
runners.spotlight: Returns to this surface after string of good efforts on turf, but probably needs to find something extra to overcome current mark and was well beaten over C&D on dirt debut in October.
runners.comment: Consistent on turf this season but on fairly tough mark and not yet proven on dirt

runners.name: Romantic Son
runners.spotlight: In excellent form on turf this season (three wins) and continued the good work when third over C&D on last month's dirt debut; still unexposed on this surface; high on the list.
runners.comment: Three-time turf winner this season; placed on last month's dirt debut; high on the list

runners.name: Victory Sky
runners.spotlight: Prevailed by a neck over C&D last month and has now won three times on dirt this season; might still be on the upgrade and Nichola Yuen claims a very useful 10lb today.
runners.comment: Prevailed by a neck over C&D last month and has now won three times on dirt this season

races.race_name: CAA Stellar Lily Agnes EBF Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hickory Lad
runners.spotlight: Made all to beat two rivals at Musselburgh (5f, good) last week, going one better than he did at Thirsk; faces competition for the lead to his inside but is otherwise one of the main form contenders.
runners.comment: Made all at Musselburgh last week, building on his Thirsk effort

runners.name: Final Appeal
runners.spotlight: Already proven on a turning, left-handed track having recorded a comfortable win from the front at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) to build on his Nottingham effort; looks a very suitable type.
runners.comment: Gained his AW win at left-handed, turning Wolverhampton; looks a very suitable type

runners.name: Wait Geordie
runners.spotlight: Notable contender, being a once-raced novice winner who represents a local yard that targets Chester; made all at Bath (5f, good) and should be handy from his low draw; commands respect.
runners.comment: Once-raced novice winner (at Bath) who represents a local yard that targets Chester

runners.name: Adonius
runners.spotlight: Two from two, namely wins at Musselburgh (5f, soft/good to soft) by 7l then a neck; made all and always doing enough under a penalty last time; big player from the inside gate.
runners.comment: Two from two, namely wins at Musselburgh; big player from the inside gate

runners.name: Black Treasure
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros yearling; by Blackbeard; related to a few 2yo scorers (7f/1m) but inexperience may count against her, being the sole newcomer in the field; check the betting for further guidance.
runners.comment: 30,000euros yearling; sole newcomer in the field; heed the market signals

runners.name: Cailin Aine
runners.spotlight: Showed ability with third-place finish at Redcar (5f, good to firm) but recorded only an ordinary RPR; drawn wider than the main form contenders and faces a difficult task.
runners.comment: Recorded only an ordinary RPR in finishing third at Redcar

runners.name: Fern Clyde
runners.spotlight: Seemed to need the outing/experience at Beverley (5f, good to soft) where she finished sixth; however, needs a big step forward in terms of form and has the worst of the draw.
runners.comment: Needs to step up plenty on her Beverley effort

runners.name: Yahaira
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form, having posted a duck egg at Bath (5f, good); perhaps needed the run but this looks a stiff assignment.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form, having posted a duck egg at Bath

races.race_name: Kauto The King Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Etalon
runners.spotlight: Fitting of cheekpieces sparked a revival in December, when landing a fifth success over fences at Ascot (2m3f, good to soft); followed that up with a career-best RPR when third off this mark there (2m5f) the following month; respected but he'll need to leave behind a lesser effort last time.
runners.comment: Ascot win and good third since the cheekpieces went on; below par latest but respected.

runners.name: Western General
runners.spotlight: Back to form after a second wind op, posting career-best RPRs when scoring at Chepstow in February and when runner-up at Hereford the following month (both at 2m); has his best form over shorter but shapes as though this trip should suit and he commands respect on that recent form.
runners.comment: Back to form post wind op; up in trip but respected judged on recent good form.

runners.name: Somespring Special
runners.spotlight: 4-15 over fences; gained a win at Chepstow (extended 2m3f, good) in October but hit with a 7lb rise and she's been well held on both outings this year, most recently trailing home last of three at Wincanton (2m, good) three weeks ago; only 2lb lower and now has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 4-15 over fences; below par twice this year; only 2lb lower and has something to prove.

runners.name: Centara
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive over fences last summer, winning five handicaps (four here) at around 2m on ground no worse than good to soft, though he ended the campaign pulled up at Cheltenham; has since had a second wind op and could find more at this longer trip (2m3f hurdle winner), which will be needed off this mark following a break.
runners.comment: Five chase wins last summer; second wind op; steps up in trip but needs to find more.

runners.name: Light N Strike
runners.spotlight: Progressive for Emma Lavelle last term, winning this off today's mark and backing it up with solid placed efforts; however, he's struggled for this yard (early unseat in Kerry National, pulled up at Chepstow and tailed off at Cheltenham when last seen in October) and now has something to prove.
runners.comment: Won this off today's mark last year but form for this stable has been poor.

runners.name: Hercule Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: Yet to make an impact in three starts for this yard this year, but he showed some smart form in Ireland for Willie Mullins, notably landing a 2m4f Grade 3 at around this time last season; looks attractively treated on that evidence; one to watch in the market, though a return to form is needed.
runners.comment: Grade 3 winner (2m4f) for Willie Mullins last year but offered little as yet for new yard.

runners.name: Ventura Highway
runners.spotlight: Ex-French; gained a chase success at Auteuil (2m6f) last May but hasn't matched that since; current ability is a little tricky to pin down and he looks to have something to find on RPRs on this British debut.
runners.comment: Ex-French; Auteuil winner last May; below par since; bit to prove on UK debut.

races.race_name: Join Ladbrokes Bet 5 Get 30 Earl Grosvenor Handicap

runners.name: Percy's Lad
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer with a very good Chester record, including a win in this race on good to firm in 2024 (also third on soft in 2023); off for 20 months prior to finishing well held at the Newmarket Craven meeting three weeks ago but he led for long enough to think plenty of ability remains and this has presumably been the target; could bounce back to form with a bang.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024; led for long way on comeback run; could be primed for another bold bid

runners.name: Lir Speciale
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign with a heavy-ground 7f double at Doncaster; underlined how well he operates on Town Moor with a good third of 22 in the Spring Mile (good to soft) on reappearance in March; claims if he can reproduce that sort of form at Chester (first visit).
runners.comment: Two 7f Doncaster wins in mud in late 2025; also good third of 22 there on 1m reappearance

runners.name: Divine Libra
runners.spotlight: Has gained both handicap wins at Chester, including over the bare 7f at this meeting (good) in May 2024; respectable fifth in this last year; a reappearance run over 6f at the Newmarket Craven meeting could have put him spot on for this; likely to be in the mix here again.
runners.comment: Good Chester record includes 7f win at this meeting in 2024; should be sharper for return

runners.name: Snow Master
runners.spotlight: Impressed when winning two fast-ground novices at Yarmouth (7f/1m) just over a year apart; too keen when sixth to Zeus Olympios in 1m Group races at Haydock (led) and Newmarket (held up) in the autumn; has switched from Tom Dascombe to Andrew Balding ahead of reappearance (same owners); also gelded which could help to settle him down; Double Rush's exploits this spring underline the yard's prowess with new recruits and this one has similar potential.
runners.comment: Promising for Tom Dascombe and interesting new recruit for top yard having been gelded

runners.name: God Of War
runners.spotlight: Never involved back on turf at Newmarket last month; however, he returned with two respectable efforts on AW prior to that and the fact he's dipped to 1lb below the mark he defied at Sandown (7f, soft) last summer and was runner-up at this meeting last year make him a dangerous one to discount.
runners.comment: Below par latest but on winning mark and second at this meeting last year; can't discount

runners.name: Partisan Hero
runners.spotlight: All wins have been over 7f and 1m so it arguably bodes well for his prospects here that he's performed well over sprint trips in Bahrain this year; very good second in this race (good ground) last year from an outside stall and this front-runner has fared a lot better with the draw this time; top of the list.
runners.comment: Second in this from wide stall in 2025 and this front-runner is much better drawn now

runners.name: Bellarchi
runners.spotlight: Last four wins at Musselburgh, including two last month, latterly producing a career-best performance to see off ten rivals over 1m on good; runner-up on only Chester visit; a further 4lb rise demands even more but can't rule out in her current mood.
runners.comment: In career-best form with two Musselburgh wins last month; can't rule out in current mood

runners.name: Thunder Roar
runners.spotlight: Very useful handicapper who added to his tally with a 1m win at Ayr (soft) last September; ended that campaign with an excellent second at York but not at his best in two comeback outings in March; third on his previous visit to Chester but will do well to match or better that from the outside stall.
runners.comment: Very useful but below par both runs this year and has the outside stall

runners.name: The Angel King
runners.spotlight: Useful dual 7f winner (both on ground slower than good) in France but well held on recent 7f Haydock stable debut; needs to have come on a lot in a short time.
runners.comment: Useful dual 7f winner in France but no obvious promise on recent yard debut

runners.name: Brighton Boy
runners.spotlight: Had a below-par Al Shabab Storm in behind when winning a useful C&D seller (soft) for Andrew Balding last September; made a respectable start for new connections when seventh of 12 over 7f at Thirsk (good) 18 days ago; entitled to come on for the outing but the draw hasn't done him any favours here.
runners.comment: C&D winner; should come on for recent yard debut but stall 12 is far from ideal.

runners.name: Supido
runners.spotlight: Fifth over C&D (good) at this meeting last year on his first outing for Ian Williams and followed that with a very good fifth of 30 in the Britannia at Royal Ascot (1m, good to firm); failed to match that level later in the campaign but his mark has dipped and dangerous if bouncing back to form after a winter break; a good draw and Billy Loughnane in the saddle are positives.
runners.comment: Well treated on form early last season; has good draw and B Loughnane riding; contender

runners.name: City House
runners.spotlight: Smart turf form at up to in Bahrain for George Scott early last year; best run for Adrian McGuinness when second over 6f at Dundalk in February and bounced back from a lesser effort at Newcastle on Good Friday when fifth of 12 on return to turf at Naas (6f, good) last Monday; has a handy draw but not obviously well treated on recent exploits.
runners.comment: Creditable fifth on turf latest but more needed here; does have a handy draw, though

runners.name: Solar Aclaim
runners.spotlight: Useful sprinter, landing soft-ground wins over 6f here and 5f at Yarmouth last summer; has had wind surgery ahead of reappearance; a new trip and a wide stall and are enough reasons to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: 6f course winner but new trip and wide stall enough reasons to oppose; has had wind op

runners.name: Al Shabab Storm
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Listed race here (good to soft) for Andrew Balding in 2024; still capable of smart performances for Marco Botti last year but wasn't at his best on final two starts, including in a course seller; sold for 45,000gns in October; a Group 1 entry at Royal Ascot suggests new connections are hopeful he retains all his ability.
runners.comment: Course Listed winner at peak but ended 2025 out of form; new yard

races.race_name: Weatherbys Cheshire Oaks (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Amelia Earhart
runners.spotlight: Started favourite in all five juvenile appearances, culminating in an easy success in Leopardstown maiden (1m, yielding) that was won 12 months earlier by the same yard's Minnie Hauk, who followed up in this race en route to even bigger things; sister to a Chester Cup winner for her trainer, whose Cheshire Oaks record is impressive; brings strong credentials and has to be feared, assuming she takes to first-time hood/blinkers.
runners.comment: Ballydoyle filly; has strong credentials based on her Leopardstown win and her pedigree
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Sugar Island
