runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly whose 2yo campaign was bookended by Curragh wins, latest comfortably in Group 3 contest over 1m on heavy ground; the form pick even with a 5lb penalty, albeit some of today's rivals (including stablemate Amelia Earhart) could reach or surpass her level.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner; the form pick but some of today's rivals could reach or surpass her level
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: I'm The One
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred filly, by Sea The Stars and the first foal of a German 1m4f Group 1 winner; impressively justified favouritism in Newbury maiden (1m2f, good) last month for a highly promising debut, striding clear in the final 1f to score by 6l; very bright prospect who is currently ante-post favourite for the Oaks and commands major respect.
runners.comment: Impressive debut performance in Newbury maiden; favourite for the Oaks; highly respected

runners.name: Winged One
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward to win well at Newcastle (1m, AW) in the second of her two autumn starts; however, she's the only runner who isn't entered in the Oaks and, as her bare form backs up, perhaps brings the least ability and/or potential.
runners.comment: Big step forward to win on AW but perhaps brings the least ability and/or potential

runners.name: A La Prochaine
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega filly who cost 550,000gns as a yearling; appeared to win with something in hand at Newbury (1m, soft) in sole 2yo start; promising sort who could make her mark at this higher level, albeit rather lacks the dazzle of the other once-raced contender, I'm The One.
runners.comment: Appeared to win with something in hand at Newbury in sole 2yo start; promising sort

races.race_name: Boodles Chester Vase Stakes (Group 3) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Benvenuto Cellini
runners.spotlight: Made all in a maiden last July and most impressive in four-runner Group 2 at Leopardstown (1m, good to yielding) in September; made favourite for the Derby on the back of that, and still has that position despite his third of five behind two stablemates when warm favourite for Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m) in October, which was on heavy ground; sister Giselle won last year's Lingfield Oaks Trial (Oaks fifth); their trainer is the first one to turn to for winners of these Chester Derby and Oaks trials; hard to beat.
runners.comment: Most impressive in 1m Group 2 at Leopardstown in September; Group 1 Futurity 3rd on heavy

runners.name: Del Maro
runners.spotlight: Derby entry; progressive as 2yo (hooded three times) while always finding one or two too good for him, in the last instance when third behind two Aidan O'Brien-trained colts in Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm); it's unlikely he had to reproduce that form when seeing off a newcomer by half a length in novice at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) on reappearance, at 4-7; not the most exciting on that evidence but likely to relish this extra distance.
runners.comment: Off the mark on reappearance; best form when third in Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f) as 2yo

runners.name: Mr Colonel
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck on first two runs before his front-running and rallying fifth of eight in Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm; sole run in cheekpieces) in October, just over 2l behind third-placed Del Maro; clear before halfway when 11-10 for a four-runner novice at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) 16 days ago but had to battle for a short-head win; while he still looks a work in progress and 1m4f+ is likely to suit, he'd be a surprise winner of this.
runners.comment: Just over 2l behind Del Maro on final 2yo start; short-head win on return; 1m4f+ can suit

runners.name: Mr Vettori
runners.spotlight: Irish Derby entry whose dam is sister to Yorkshire Oaks and Vermeille winner Warm Heart; hung markedly right on his last two starts as a 2yo when 50-1 second at the Curragh (good) to subsequent Group 1 Futurity winner Hawk Mountain and going one better in another 1m maiden at Gowran (heavy);  made a satisfactory return when second of four in a conditions event at Cork (10.5f, good) 12 days ago; can build on that but he needs to does so in a major way.
runners.comment: Useful middle-distance prospect, at the least, but this race demands a marked step forward

runners.name: Proposition
runners.spotlight: Has big-race entries; Frankel close relation to multiple Group 1-winning fillies Magical (up to 1m4f) and Rhododendron (up to 1m2f); well held on debut but second favourite for a maiden at Galway (7f, soft to heavy) later in October and eventually got the better of an odds-on stablemate who's not been seen since; cheekpieces go on and he's presumably the stable second string, but surely set for big improvement over middle distances this term.
runners.comment: Galway 2yo maiden winner (7f, soft to heavy); surely set for big improvement; cheekpieces
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

races.race_name: American Holidays Handicap

runners.name: Alaminos
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt whose form features a debut success last August and neck second, looking unlucky, at Pontefract (6f, good; had traffic issues) on handicap/seasonal debut; remains open to further improvement and commands respect.
runners.comment: Unlucky second at Pontefract on handicap/seasonal debut; still open to further improvement
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Watcha Snoop
runners.spotlight: Made all at Ascot (6f, soft) to build on debut effort; subsequently down the field in the Mill Reef and a valuable race on Champions Day but may be capable of progress back down in class; local runner who is interesting on handicap debut.
runners.comment: May be capable of progress back down in class; interesting on handicap debut

runners.name: Tricky Tel
runners.spotlight: Career commenced with two wins at Chester (5.5f/6f, good) last May; not in the same form elsewhere subsequently but the return to this venue, combined with a gelding operation, may prompt a resurgence; one of three runners for his local stable.
runners.comment: Return to Chester may prompt a resurgence; 2-2 here, both starts last May

runners.name: Ruby's Angel
runners.spotlight: Absent since a below-par effort switched to AW (7f) eight months ago; opened her account in competitive nursery at the York Ebor festival (6f, good to firm; led final strides) the time before and may be capable of better still on turf; this local runner is shortlisted.
runners.comment: Won competitive nursery at the York Ebor festival in last turf attempt; likely player

runners.name: Angel Love
runners.spotlight: Comfortably justified favouritism at left-handed, turning Wolverhampton (kept to 6f on AW) in final 2yo start, stepping up on her placed efforts; interesting contender on turf/handicap debut, being one who could take well to Chester and improve further.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in final 2yo start; could take well to Chester and improve further

runners.name: Temple Of Athena
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 5f, winning three times, including at Chester (good to soft); not disgraced in Listed event at Bath most recently; well drawn but the return to 6f presents a question mark.
runners.comment: Three-time 5f winner, including at Chester; return to 6f presents a question mark

runners.name: Cherry Baker
runners.spotlight: Returns to a more realistic level on handicap debut, having faced a stiff task (well beaten) in the Nell Gwyn on reappearance; sole 6f attempt resulted in ready win at Southwell (AW) in final 2yo outing; unexposed over this distance.
runners.comment: Sole 6f attempt resulted in ready win at Southwell; unexposed over this distance

runners.name: Arduis Invicta
runners.spotlight: Campaigned at Listed level, with mixed results, since scoring off 13lb lower in last handicap attempt; peak figure may not be reliable and she needs to prove current mark is appropriate.
runners.comment: Peak figure at Listed level may not be reliable; 13lb higher than last handicap attempt

runners.name: Star Material
runners.spotlight: Best form when winning a Ripon novice event (5f, good) and second in Ayr nursery (6f, soft); chance of emerging on top in this competitive handicap partly depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance of winning partly depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Go Vince Go
runners.spotlight: Has shown no further progress since the last of his three juvenile wins and some of today's rivals are less exposed; perhaps needed his Thirsk reappearance but this looks a difficult task.
runners.comment: Has shown no further progress since the last of his three juvenile wins

runners.name: Do Bronxs
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after his winning debut last term; possibly flattered by apparent peak effort (last of five in Irish Group 1) and has something to prove off his mark in this handicap debut; gelded since last run.
runners.comment: Possibly flattered by apparent peak effort; something to prove off his mark

runners.name: Evie Ross
runners.spotlight: Has raced three times, all last August, clear best form when scoring at Chepstow (good to firm) in sole 5f attempt; something to prove on the trip front but market support should be noted.
runners.comment: Something to prove on the trip front but market support should be noted
runners.owner: S R Hope And S W Barrow

races.race_name: Weatherbys Cheshire Oaks (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.name: Amelia Earhart
runners.spotlight: Started favourite in all five juvenile appearances, culminating in an easy success in Leopardstown maiden (1m, yielding) that was won 12 months earlier by the same yard's Minnie Hauk, who followed up in this race en route to even bigger things; sister to a Chester Cup winner for her trainer, whose Cheshire Oaks record is impressive; brings strong credentials and has to be feared, assuming she takes to first-time hood/blinkers.
